title: Rebaseline request: MAP wedge and procurement sprint
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
To: Alyssa Green <alyssa@medapp.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Rebaseline request: MAP wedge and procurement sprint

Alyssa —

Quick note after the sync this morning. We heard your procurement team has a short hold on budget approvals that will likely push the originals out by ~2–3 weeks. That impacts the MAP milestone on our side (integration go/no-go) and the planned eval window.

Proposed immediate steps from Redwood:
- Shift the MAP milestones by +14 days as a baseline (see items below).
- Open a procurement sprint with your team to unblock the purchase order (Ben can lead).
- Short canary eval (week-of) to validate integration path while procurement finalizes.

I attached a draft MAP_v2 (worksheet 1 = milestones, worksheet 2 = ownership). Can you confirm the expected procurement clearance date from your team by EOD tomorrow? Once we have that, we can firm up the updated cutover and canary schedule.

Thanks —
Camila Reyes
Account Lead, Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.ai
Redwood Inference

Attachments: MAP_v2.xlsx (workbook, 120KB)


From: Alyssa Green <alyssa@medapp.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement Team <procure@medapp.com>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Rebaseline request: MAP wedge and procurement sprint

Camila —

Thanks for the crisp note. Confirming: procurement paused approvals this morning due to a budget reforecast. We expect an answer from Finance by end of next week (May 28).

Impact for MedApp: if we slip >2 weeks, our internal launch windows for the pilot cohort shift and we need a tighter mitigation plan for the canary so we don't miss Q3 commitments.

Can Ben connect with our procurement lead (cc'd) to get an interim list of open paperwork? If Redwood can propose a tightened eval/canary that runs independently of final purchase (limited scope, gated access), that will help keep momentum.

Alyssa
VP, Product — MedApp Inc.
alyssa@medapp.com

--
Procurement note: finance@medapp.com will confirm revised budget timeline.


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
To: Alyssa Green <alyssa@medapp.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Rebaseline request: MAP wedge and procurement sprint

Alyssa —

Appreciate the clarity. I updated the MAP and added a procurement timeline appendix (procurement_timeline.pdf) — attached. High level proposal:

1) Rebaseline (baseline +14 days):
   - Integration window: Jun 7 — Jun 18
   - Canary eval window (limited dataset): Jun 14 — Jun 18
   - Full acceptance testing: Jun 21 — Jun 25

2) Contingency lane: if Finance pushes beyond May 28, we execute a reduced-scope canary (edge routing only) Jun 14 — Jun 16 to validate model endpoints and latency metrics.

3) Ask of MedApp: procurement to circulate a short list of remaining PO items today so Ben can triage with your procurement lead. If Procurement confirms step-by-step clearance by May 28 we keep the +14 baseline. If not, we will execute contingency lane and request an exec-level decision on the launch date.

I pulled in Jonas to keep the operations side synced. He will own the canary runbook and the KPI checklist.

Camila Reyes
Account Lead, Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.ai
Attachments: MAP_v2.xlsx, procurement_timeline.pdf


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 16:40:00 -0700
Subject: Escalation: MedApp procurement hold — potential close slip and exec ask

Kimberly / Markus —

Thread summary below (Alyssa/MedApp). Procurement paused approvals; Finance expects to respond by May 28. If Finance misses the May 28 target, we expect at minimum a 2–3 week slip in the MAP and a higher likelihood of a formal launch replan.

Why this matters: the customer has external launch dependencies (pilot cohort) tied to calendar Q3; missing the current window reduces trust and could push the deal to a later slate. We recommend the following escalation steps now:
1) Approve a one-time uplift credit (up to 7.5% of ARR) as negotiation flexibility if MedApp requests contractual concessions tied to timeline slippage.
2) Authorize a dedicated procurement liaison (Ben) timebox of 8 hours/week to accelerate PO items.
3) Prep an exec-level decision brief (1-pager) to present to MedApp on May 27 if Finance hasn't cleared.

Attached: quoted thread and the MAP_v2 we shared.

If Procurement confirms step-by-step clearance by May 28 we keep the +14 baseline. If not, we will execute contingency lane and request an exec-level decision on the launch date.
>
> I pulled in Jonas to keep the operations side synced. He will own the canary runbook and the KPI checklist.
>
> Camila Reyes
> Account Lead, Redwood Inference
> camila@redwood.ai


From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>
To: Alyssa Green <alyssa@medapp.com>, Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 22 May 2027 15:46:00 -0700
Subject: Finalized rebaseline & next steps (MAP RID-2027-0419)

Alyssa / team —

Thanks for the coordination. Per MedApp's confirmation from Finance earlier today, the procurement hold is cleared with stepwise sign-off (PO issuance expected 2027-05-28). Based on that, we are moving forward with the +14 baseline. Summary of next steps:

- Integration window: Jun 7 — Jun 18 (team: Redwood Eng, MedApp Integrations)
- Canary eval: Jun 14 — Jun 18 (Jonas owns runbook; redwood-runbook-v1 attached in Drive)
- Acceptance testing: Jun 21 — Jun 25
- Procurement: PO issuance target 2027-05-28 (MedApp Finance)
- DocuSign for updated SOW sent: https://app.redwood.com/docusign/RID-2027-0419

I've attached the final MAP_v2 and a short one-page exec brief. Ben, continue procurement liaison work; Camila, please keep Kimberly/Markus looped on any customer negotiations re: concessions.

Jonas Weber
Head of Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference
jonas@redwood.ai

Attachments referenced: final_map_signed_link.txt (pointer to signed doc), redwood-runbook-v1 (Drive link)

title: Assimilation bundle: partner contracting & escalation matrix for vector stores
preview:
From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha@pinecone.io>, Alex DeVries <alex@weaviate.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Assimilation bundle: partner contracting & escalation matrix for vector stores

Hi Samantha / Alex —

Thanks again for the productive sync earlier. We walked away with a clear set of asks from both sides: Pinecone wants a concise SOW that maps operational responsibilities; Weaviate asked for clarity on the fallback routing and data residency clauses; and both teams requested an escalation matrix so field ops know who to loop in when index inconsistencies or cross-store failovers happen.

To move this to contracting + ops alignment, I've attached a proposed \"assimilation matrix\" (Redwood_Assimilation_Matrix_v1.xlsx) and a short SOW stub for Pinecone. Ask to the group: can you each review and call out non-starters by Friday?

Specifically, Redwood will own: model-to-index mapping config, SDK compatibility tests, and prefix/KV cache invalidation coordination. Partner asks to own: index failover SLA, cold-tier pull performance guarantees, and a documented debug runbook.

Next steps I propose:
- Internal review (Ben/Marcus/Laura): confirm Redwood responsibilities and owner names in the matrix by Wed EOD.
- Partner review (Samantha/Alex): mark redlines and operational gaps in the doc by Fri.
- If redlines exceed 3 substantive items, we schedule a contracts touchpoint next week.

Attachments: Redwood_Assimilation_Matrix_v1.xlsx (ops/roles), Initial_SOW_Pinecone_draft.pdf (commercial + SLAs), Ops_Escalation_Roles.docx (template)

Thanks —
Vanessa Ortiz
Head of Partnerships, Redwood Inference
vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0148

---
(Internal note: I want to keep both Pinecone & Weaviate in scope but need concrete SLA language on cold-tiering and signing authority for runbook updates.)

From: Samantha Lee <samantha@pinecone.io>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Assimilation bundle: partner contracting & escalation matrix for vector stores

Vanessa —

Appreciate the clear ask and the docs. Quick read-through from our side (Pinecone):

- SOW: Our legal has a few standard clauses they will redline (liability cap tied to usage, data residency addendum for EU customers). We'll circle back with exact language.
- Ops SLA: We can commit to a 99.9% index availability for hot indexes and provide target 95th-percentile fetch latency for hot queries. Cold-tier pull performance is trickier; we propose a \"best-effort + throughput budget\" model rather than hard p99 SLA for cold pulls.
- Escalation matrix: happy to populate names for Tier 1/2 on our side; we will need Redwood's on-call rotation for crosswalk.

Quick questions:
1) Is Redwood planning to run the prefix/KV cache on Hosted or Dedicated? That affects where we expect cache invalidations to originate.
2) For the SOW, who at Redwood signs with partner vendors — legal or partnerships? We need a signing authority listed to accelerate DocuSign routing.

I'll push a marked-up SOW and an ops-sheet by Friday. In parallel I'll loop our contracts lead (cc'd offline) if we hit a sticking point.

Best,
Samantha Lee
Head of Strategic Alliances, Pinecone
samantha@pinecone.io

---
> On Tue, May 18, 2027 at 9:12 AM Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > Thanks again for the productive sync earlier....

(attachment: Pinecone_SOW_comments_placeholder.txt)

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 08:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Assimilation bundle: partner contracting & escalation matrix for vector stores

Vanessa — quick internal alignment.

High-level comments after my pass on the matrix and SOW:
- Ops owners: suggest we list both a primary and an escalation owner (e.g., Marcus Lin (primary), Laura Bennett (escalation)) for each row to reduce ambiguity.
- Caching: Redwood will operate prefix/KV cache on Dedicated pools for now; we'll add a callout for a future pilot on Hosted. That answers Samantha's Q1.
- Signing authority: Product Partnerships can sign NDAs and pilot SOWs under $50k; anything commercial / commitment should go to Legal for signature. We should surface this in the SOW doc (signature_block: Partnerships or Legal).
- Cold-tier wording: recommend \"throughput budget + target latency range\" rather than hard SLA. Partners will accept that more readily and it maps to how S3/backing stores behave.

I've updated Redwood_Assimilation_Matrix_v1.xlsx with owner columns and a small 'signoff_threshold' column. Pushed to the Drive link in the thread.

I'll be sitting with Marcus this afternoon to prepare talking points for the Friday redline call.

-b

Ben Carter
Director, Partner Engineering, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.ai

---
> On Wed, May 19, 2027 at 11:03 AM Samantha Lee <samantha@pinecone.io> wrote:
> > Vanessa — Appreciate the clear ask and the docs....

From: Alex DeVries <alex@weaviate.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>, Samantha Lee <samantha@pinecone.io>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Meghan Torres <meghan@vectoradvisors.com>
Date: Sat, 22 May 2027 15:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Assimilation bundle: partner contracting & escalation matrix for vector stores

Hi all —

Thanks for the loop. Weaviate had a quick read and a couple of asks to make the ops handoff smooth:

1) Index schema mapping: request a single canonical JSON schema per index that both sides will version. Weaviate will accept semantic versioning bumps (v1.0 -> v1.1) rather than free-for-all migrations.
2) Fallback routing: we'll accept the \"model-level preference -> region -> cold-tier\" policy, but want explicit fallback timeout windows in the SOW (e.g., switch to remote index after 250ms p95 fail).
3) Debug artifacts: Weaviate needs an agreed log format for cross-store traces (request_id, model_id, index_id, timestamp, trace_flags).

I've attached a short template for index schema (Index_Canonical_Template.json) and a proposed fallback timeout table. If Redwood agrees to the schema/versioning model and the timeout windows, we'll be comfortable signing the runbook commitment.

I'll be on PTO next week but available for a 30-minute signoff on Tuesday morning if needed.

Cheers,
Alex DeVries
Partner Solutions, Weaviate
alex@weaviate.com

---
> On Thu, May 20, 2027 at 8:46 AM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > Vanessa — quick internal alignment....

(attachment: Index_Canonical_Template.json)

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha@pinecone.io>, Alex DeVries <alex@weaviate.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Meghan Torres <meghan@vectoradvisors.com>
Date: Sat, 22 May 2027 15:16:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Assimilation bundle: partner contracting & escalation matrix for vector stores

Thanks everyone — quick wrap and agreed next steps: 

- Redwood (Ben/Marcus) will finalize owners and update the Matrix with primary/escalation contacts by Mon EOD (internal).
- Samantha will push Pinecone's marked SOW and liability language by Fri; we will triage redlines and escalate to legal only if they exceed the signoff threshold Ben noted.
- Alex's schema/versioning ask: Redwood agrees to canonical JSON schema + semantic versioning. Marcus to add \"version\" column to the matrix and link to the Drive schema template.
- Cold-tier: agree on throughput-budget language in SOW (no hard p99). We'll add a fallback timeout table with target windows (250ms p95 for fallback trigger) — Marcus to draft.
- Ops runbook: Meghan (cc'd) will help synthesize the runbook template and map roles to both partner and Redwood on-call rotations.

I'll schedule a 30-minute redline sync for next Tues 10:30AM PT if partners are available. If either partner can't do Tue, please propose an alternate.

Thanks — appreciate the rapid turnaround. Looking forward to a clean SOW and a pragmatic ops agreement.

Vanessa Ortiz
Head of Partnerships, Redwood Inference
vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0148

(attachment: Redwood_Assimilation_Matrix_v1_update.xlsx)

title: Trial verification checklist + fallback / safety checks for OrbitHealth MAP
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Lambert <sarah.lambert@orbithealth.com>; Ian Mercer <ian.mercer@orbithealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Draft MAP: verification checklist + proposed safety/fallbacks (OrbitHealth trial)

Hi Sarah / Ian —

Attached is a first-pass MAP focused on what we need to verify during the 4-week trial and the minimum exit conditions that will signal a green light to move to production. High level:

- Goal: Demonstrate reliable inference for clinical triage workflows at target throughput (3–5 TPS) with P95 latency and quality thresholds met for the sample prompts you shared.
- Acceptance tests: a short list (functional smoke, throughput/latency, accuracy sampling, security evidence). Details in the attached sheet (Orbit_MAP_v1.xlsx).
- Safety / fallback: recommended automatic fallback to smaller model + cached responses on degradation, and an emergency throttle/disable switch for the UI.

Key asks from your side:
1) Confirm the representative prompt set (we used the 220-case CSV Ian provided).
2) Confirm the SLA decision date (we put June 30 as target).

Timeline suggestion: kickoff week of May 24, smoke tests May 25–27, full ramp May 28–June 24, decision meeting week of June 28.

Attachments: Orbit_MAP_v1.xlsx, security_evidence_summary.pdf

Next steps: If this matches what you expect I’ll lock the acceptance tests and assign owners.

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0142

--
Redwood Inference — production-first inference for teams


From: Sarah Lambert <sarah.lambert@orbithealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Ian Mercer <ian.mercer@orbithealth.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft MAP: verification checklist + proposed safety/fallbacks (OrbitHealth trial)

Thanks Ava — this looks helpful. A couple of clarifying questions before we sign off on the plan: 

On Fri, May 20, 2027 at 9:12 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Sarah / Ian —
> 
> Attached is a first-pass MAP focused on what we need to verify during the 4-week trial ...

1) Latency / throughput: you reference 3–5 TPS. Our internal goal is to see sustained 5 TPS at P95 < 300ms for inference (not including network). Will you be able to run that from your region?
2) Security evidence: can you provide the specific items we should expect in the "security_evidence_summary.pdf"? Our security team requires SOC2 Type II report or a third-party pen test summary.
3) Acceptance sampling: who provides the ground truth for the accuracy sampling? We may provide a labeled subset (50 examples) but want to confirm how to run comparisons and what pass thresholds look like.

We’re targeting a decision by June 30 but would prefer to keep earlier checkpoints if tests are green. Also want to ensure the rollback / throttle flows are documented in the MAP.

Thanks —
Sarah Lambert
Director of Clinical AI Operations
OrbitHealth
sarah.lambert@orbithealth.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Lambert <sarah.lambert@orbithealth.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 May 2027 16:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft MAP: verification checklist + proposed safety/fallbacks (OrbitHealth trial)

Sarah — quick answers and proposed concrete acceptance tests.

Thanks for the callouts — copying the key points into acceptance criteria so we can be explicit: 

Acceptance tests (pass/fail):
- Throughput / latency (owner: Redwood infra team / Ben): run 30-minute ramp at target workload (5 TPS sustained). Success = P95 inference latency < 300ms averaged over three runs, 99.9% tokens produced without runtime errors. Test runs documented in the MAP and results uploaded to the shared drive.
- Functional smoke (owner: Customer test lead / Ian): 25 representative prompts from your set; pass if model outputs match label at acceptable confidence and no critical hallucinations as determined by manual review.
- Accuracy sampling (owner: OrbitHealth; Redwood assists): Orbit provides a labeled 50-example holdout. We'll run batch evaluation and produce confusion/precision/recall; pass if F1 >= 0.78 on the primary clinical tag and false positive rate < 6%.
- Security evidence (owner: Redwood security + OrbitHealth procurement): we can provide SOC2 Type II summary (redacted) and the last 12 months vulnerability scan report; pen test summary available upon request under NDA. See security_evidence_summary.pdf for current artifacts.

Safety / fallback test steps (operational verification):
1) Canary rollback: simulate a degraded variant (load + simulated failures) and verify auto-fallback engages to the cached model variant within 90s.
2) Emergency throttle: trigger a soft-limit breach and confirm UI-level throttle message and that in-flight requests are drained without data loss.
3) Audit logs: confirm event records for model swaps and throttle actions are present and indexed into the instance’s audit log (sample queries included).

Deliverables and owners:
- MAP spreadsheet updated with test scripts (Ben)
- Security packet collated (Marissa)
- Test schedule and runbook (Ava + Ian)

I’ll add these as explicit rows in the MAP and share an updated version by EOD.

— Ben
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com



From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 22 May 2027 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft MAP: verification checklist + proposed safety/fallbacks (OrbitHealth trial)

Team — quick managerial note and approval to proceed.

I reviewed Ben's criteria and agree with the thresholds. A couple of commercial / operational points to lock into the MAP before we circulate externally: 
- Fallback SLA: define the cadence for notifications when fallback is engaged (immediate alert to OrbitHealth SRE + 15m follow-up with remediation plan).
- Canary window: 2-hour canary during ramp with rollback if any of the acceptance metrics fall outside agreed tolerances.
- Price addendum: we will honor the proposed trial pricing for the first month; attach final pricing proposal (pricing_proposal_v3.pdf). Any additional extended trial work will be scoped and billed.

Ava — please fold these into the MAP and the master runbook. Ben / Monica, coordinate on the security packet (Marissa will review and approve for sharing).

Marissa Cole
Head of Sales Operations, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

Hi all — attaching the first-pass artifacts for intake (see attachment stubs below). Quick notes inline.

Attachment stubs included:
- redwood_control_plane_fencing_v1.pdf (diagram, 3 pages) — shows control plane endpoints on a separate management VPC, cross-region routing overlay, and the automatic fallback route to pooled Dedicated capacity. Annotations include: KMS boundary (customer-managed keys for Private), control-plane API endpoints (mutually TLS authenticated), and control-plane DNS isolation.
- vpc_enclave_connectivity_diagram.png — shows per-tenant enclave placement, ingress points, prefix/KV cache boundaries, and egress NAT paths to customer-configured peering or transit gateway.
- segregation_matrix_table.xlsx — row per tenant class (public/self-service, enterprise-shared, enterprise-dedicated) with columns for routing zone, L4/L7 constraints, logging retention, and required attestations.

Narrative excerpt (pasted):
"Redwood enforces separation between control and data planes by placing orchestration/control services into a dedicated management VPC with no direct tenant data-plane egress. For Dedicated and Private deployments, tenant inference instances run in isolated enclaves with strict egress rules: either customer-managed peer links (Private) or our dedicated egress pools (Dedicated) governed by policy. Keys for customer data are stored in KMS instances scoped per-customer for Private deployments; for Dedicated we offer optional CMEK with HSM-backed KMS integration. Audit logs for control-plane operations are retained in an immutable logging bucket with retention policy configurable per contract."

A few action items:
- Elena: Do you want packet-filter examples or are the annotated ACL callouts sufficient?
- Samir: If you can confirm the list of reviewers who need shareable copies, we’ll prepare redacted and full versions.

We’ll hand off final PDF + spreadsheet by EOD today once we confirm redaction needs.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Principal Security Architect, Redwood Inference
kimberly_park@redwood.example

title: Committed compute path & isolation — post-call recap
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Lara Chen <lara.chen@orbitalbio.com>, Procurement <procurement@orbitalbio.com>
Cc: Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Committed compute path & isolation — post-call recap

Thanks again for a productive call today — appreciate the clear description of your latency sensitivity and regulatory constraints. Quick recap and the next steps we agreed on, plus a couple of follow-ups from our side.

High-level summary (pain / goals)
- Pain: model inference variability on burst loads and noisy multi-tenant interference; current infra shows 2-3x latency spikes at peak.
- Goal: predictable token-level latency for customer-facing chat (target p95 < 150ms at steady state for 200 concurrent users; peak scaling to ~1k rps).
- Compliance: PHI-adjacent workflows require data-plane isolation and customer-controlled KMS for keys.

What we proposed (committed pool profile)
- Committed GPU pool (isolated tenant lane) with 8x A100-similar V100-equivalent slots to start; autoscaling headroom + burst buffer to handle short 10–20% traffic bursts.
- Isolation: private VPC peering, separate node pool, and optional on-prem connector for sealed workloads.
- SLA targets we iterated: 99.95% uptime for the pool; p95 generation latency target of < 150ms under provisioned throughput.

Technical constraints / assumptions from your side
- Model family: medium-sized decoder-only models (estimated 16–20B parameters) with 512 token windows.
- Expected Q3 peak: 200k tokens/day ramping to 900k in six months.

Immediate next steps (owner / ETA)
- Redwood: share a short committed-pool outline (attached) + preliminary SOW for pricing anchors — Ben (today / EOD).
- Orbital: confirm procurement contact and whether SOC2 artifact bundle is needed up-front — Lara (by Fri 5/21).
- Schedule a 60-minute technical deep-dive with infra and security to validate KV-cache behavior and network topology — propose Tue 5/25 10:00–11:00 PT.

Attachments: redwood-committed-pool-outline-v1.pdf (capacity profile, isolation options), poa-action-items.xlsx (simple checklist)

If anything above looks off or you want different SLA/throughput targets called out explicitly, say the word and we’ll adjust. Otherwise I’ll circulate the SOW anchor and calendar invite for the deep-dive.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0182

---

On Wed, 19 May 2027 14:20:00 -0700, Lara Chen <lara.chen@orbitalbio.com> wrote:
> Thanks Ben — good session.
>
> A couple clarifications: our peak estimate of ~1k rps is for short bursts tied to experiment windows (10–30m), steady traffic is much lower. We do need the KMS integration to be customer-managed; anything that stores unencrypted tokens outside our VPC is a non-starter.
>
> I’ll loop in procurement and Raj (cc’d) and confirm whether we need a formal SOC2 package up-front or whether we can get away with an attestation first.
>
> — Lara


From: Lara Chen <lara.chen@orbitalbio.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Raj Patel <raj.patel@orbitalbio.com>, Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 16:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Committed compute path & isolation — post-call recap

Ben — thanks for the recap. Confirming a couple of items from our side:

- We agree with the isolation approach (private lane + VPC peering). KMS must be customer-managed; if Redwood can support an external key provider via the usual HSM/KMS integrations, that’s perfect.
- For scaling, we’re happy with a small committed base and burst buffer, but can you include a short run-rate projection for token spend so procurement has an anchor?
- SOC2: procurement will want the full SOC2 bundle before finalizing a commitment — I’ll confirm by Friday whether an attestation is acceptable in the interim.

Also confirming: Raj (procurement) will take point on contracts — Raj, can you share the procurement email for PO routing and whether your finance team requires a specific contract clause around data residency?

Thanks — looking forward to the deep-dive.

Best,
Lara Chen
Head of Platform Engineering
Orbital BioSystems

preview:
From: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwoodinference.com>

Cc: Lukas Meyer <lukas.meyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Control-plane handshake & residency evidence for EU/APAC private topologies

Hi Anna / Paul —

Following our call this morning, attaching a short evidence request to validate EuCorp's residency constraints and to finalize the private VPC topology plan for EU and APAC. High-level asks:

- Confirm hard constraints for data-at-rest (eu region only), including any allowed transient controls in APAC for failover.
- Which systems require encryption keys to be resident in-region vs keys allowed in a centralized KMS? (Please list key aliases)
- Acceptable log retention boundaries for audit logs and tracebacks (EU site vs central collection)
- Any required network peering or approved cloud providers for cross-border failover (we need the list to validate peering playbook)

I've also listed the artifacts we need to attach to the contract package: SOC 2 Type II report, KMS integration diagram, network ACL list, and a signed data- residency attestation. See attached: residency_evidence_request.docx (short checklist).

Next steps from our side: Rafael will produce the initial VPC topology PDF and Marcus will outline the control-plane handshake for failover. Can you confirm a deadline for the artifacts? We are aiming to include everything in the final SOW by 2027-06-01.

Thanks,
Naomi
Naomi Feldman
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
naomi@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0198

Attachment: residency_evidence_request.docx (docx, 28 KB)

----
From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwoodinference.com>, Anna Berg <anna.berg@eucorp.com>, Paul Saito <paul.saito@apac-enterprise.co.jp>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Lukas Meyer <lukas.meyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Control-plane handshake & residency evidence for EU/APAC private topologies

Thanks Naomi — quick update from infra/arch side.

I attached our proposed VPC topology for review (vpc-topology-eu-apac.pdf). Key points: 
- Primary data plane: EU region (single primary cluster in eu-west-1 equivalent). All customer datasets are pinned to that cluster.
- Warm-standby in APAC: compute-only nodes in ap-northeast-1 with encrypted KV cache that contains only stateless runtime caches. No persisted customer data will be written to APAC disks.
- Failover handshake: control-plane uses mutual TLS with cert pinning; failover is initiated by health checks + control-plane quorum. A preconfigured routing policy will enforce that EU-origin requests (identified by headers) always prefer EU endpoints; APAC-origin requests prefer APAC.
- KMS: we propose customer-managed keys in EU KMS for data-at-rest. For control-plane certificates and ephemeral keys, we recommend a centralized HSM in EU with cross-region read-only replication for emergency unseal (audit-controlled).

Open questions for Anna/Paul:
1) Would EuCorp accept read-only DR unlock keys replicated to APAC under strict audit and with immediate revocation capability?
2) Are there vendors (cloud or colo) you cannot accept for peering?

I'll follow up with a CSV that maps endpoints to routing policies and expected RTO/RPO.

-Rafael
Rafael Mendes
Principal Product Manager, Private Deployments | Redwood Inference
Attachment: vpc-topology-eu-apac.pdf (pdf, 450 KB)

To: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Anna Berg <anna.berg@eucorp.com>, Paul Saito <paul.saito@apac-enterprise.co.jp>

Subject: Re: Control-plane handshake & residency evidence for EU/APAC private topologies

Anna — quick operational note on the handshake and failover matrix (summary). Full CSV attached (routing-failover-matrix.csv).

- Control-plane certs: mutual TLS with cert pinning; certs stored in EU HSM. During failover, the APAC control-plane nodes use an ephemeral TLS cert signed by an emergency CA key that is read-only replicated. Emergency CA signing requires a two-person EuCorp approval (documented).
- Health checks: we use a two-tiered system — local node liveness + cross-region quorum. Failover only occurs when both local and quorum checks fail OR when EuCorp submits an approved manual failover request via our control portal.
- Data movement: no disk writes allowed in APAC; only encrypted KV cache. Logs with potential PII will be scrubbed before replication; we will publish the scrub rules.

I'll send the CSV and a short runbook describing the manual failover steps and audit hooks to the Drive link Naomi shared.

-Marcus

Hi team,

Attached is the first-pass furniture layout for Stand B12 (B12_CAD_v1.dwg). EventFurnish sent a layout and a quote but I flagged a few open questions I want us to decide on before we place the order:

- Demo counter location: currently on the left perimeter (A) but I think moving it to the front center (B) will improve sightlines for demos.
- Power lane orientation: vendor proposed a 90° run through the back; Moscone requires a main power lane with 1.5m clearance. Need to confirm routing so we can sign off on electrical work.
- Soft seating vs. bar stools: the quote includes both options. We should pick one to finalize quantities.
- Carpet color: charcoal vs. slate — marketing prefers charcoal, ops prefers slate (less visible dust).

Attachments: B12_CAD_v1.dwg (CAD), initial_layout_notes.txt (inline in drive).

Can each of you call out decisions or redlines by EOD Friday (May 21)? If we miss that window we risk the vendor’s 10-business-day lead time slipping.

Jordan

title: Joint GTM + Technical slate for Grafana resell listing (OTLP relay + Datadog Gateway)
preview:
From: Anika Bose <anika@grafana.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Partner Ops <partner-ops@datadoghq.com>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: Technical gating for marketplace listing + resale path

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the call yesterday — appreciate you walking through the resell model and the OTLP relay option. Our partner marketplace team needs a concise technical and compliance slate we can attach to the marketplace submission and share with Datadog regional ops for co-sell enablement.

Can you share:
- A short checklist of gating items (SSO, certs, encryption-in-transit + at-rest, KMS guidance)
- An integration schema for the OTLP relay (expected endpoints, auth semantics, sample config)
- Any performance/latency expectations we should surface to customers when routing from Grafana Cloud -> Redwood Dedicated -> Datadog Gateway

If possible, attach the integration schema and a concise mutual action plan (2–3 pages). We want to get the marketplace ticket filed by end of next week.

Thanks,
Anika Bose
Partner Programs 
Grafana Labs

-----
Attachment stubs: none in this message


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Anika Bose <anika@grafana.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Technical gating for marketplace listing + resale path

Anika — thanks, happy to own this. Looping in Laura (Marketplace + GTM) and Diego (Integration engineering).

Quick plan:
1) Diego will provide an integration schema and test plan (attachment).
2) Laura will draft a 1–2 page mutual action plan and outline commercial/resell language for the marketplace listing.
3) I'll coordinate the Datadog ops ask and produce a short performance note for the marketplace tile.

Target: deliver all artifacts by Thu 20 May EOD PT so you can file the ticket Friday. If that timeline is tight let us know.

Ben Carter
Head of Partner Sales
Redwood Inference

-----
Attachment stubs: planning_outline.txt (internal)


From: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Anika Bose <anika@grafana.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 08:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Technical gating for marketplace listing + resale path

Hi all,

Please find the initial technical artifacts attached and a short checklist below. I've kept the schema minimal so Grafana's marketplace team can digest quickly. Happy to expand any section.

Attachments: grafana-integration-schema.yaml (OTLP relay schema), otlp-relay-test-plan.xlsx (QA matrix)

TL;DR checklist for listing:
- Authentication: OAuth2 bearer tokens for control plane calls; mTLS optional for customer-origin traffic.
- Authorization: scoped API keys for relay ingestion with per-tenant scoping.
- Network: allowlist outbound to grafana-relay.redwood.net:443 and datadog-gw.datadoghq.com:443; provide CIDR ranges in final doc.
- Encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit; envelope encryption at rest with customer KMS supported (bring-your-own-KMS).
- Auditing: request/response trace IDs and ingestion metrics forwarded to customer's telemetry workspace for compliance; retention filter configurable per customer (default 30d).
- Performance: expected additional tail latency 20–40ms for relay path under normal load; recommend burst-capacity for high-cardinality tests.
- SSO: SAML SSO for console access; service accounts for API integrations.

Open questions for Grafana team:
1) Do you want us to publish a pre-configured OTLP Source in Grafana Cloud or just provide the endpoint and schema?
2) For the Datadog co-sell flow, partner-ops asked whether we can provide a deterministic latency SLA for Dedicated tenants — we can provide guidance but need committed GPU sizing per customer to promise numbers.

I'll be available for a 30-min technical scrub if the marketplace reviewers want to go over the schema.

— Diego
Senior Integration Engineer, Redwood Inference

-----
Attachment stubs: grafana-integration-schema.yaml, otlp-relay-test-plan.xlsx


From: Anika Bose <anika@grafana.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 20 May 2027 09:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Technical gating for marketplace listing + resale path

Diego — huge thanks, this is exactly what we need. A couple quick clarifications from the marketplace reviewers:
- They prefer a pre-configured OTLP Source (makes the onboarding flow simpler) — can you provide a sample YAML that Grafana Cloud can ingest as a template?
- On encryption at rest: they asked for a one-sentence summary we can show publicly; can you provide that line (we will not surface implementation details).
- For latency: they need conservative numbers for the public tile; please confirm we can use "expected additional tail latency +40ms" as the public guidance.

Hi all,

Please find the initial technical artifacts attached and a short checklist below. I've kept the schema minimal so Grafana's marketplace team can digest quickly. Happy to expand any section.

Attachments: grafana-integration-schema.yaml (OTLP relay schema), otlp-relay-test-plan.xlsx (QA matrix)

TL;DR checklist for listing:
- Authentication: OAuth2 bearer tokens for control plane calls; mTLS optional for customer-origin traffic.
- Authorization: scoped API keys for relay ingestion with per-tenant scoping.
- Network: allowlist outbound to grafana-relay.redwood.net:443 and datadog-gw.datadoghq.com:443; provide CIDR ranges in final doc.
- Encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit; envelope encryption at rest with customer KMS supported (bring-your-own-KMS).
- Auditing: request/response trace IDs and ingestion metrics forwarded to customer's telemetry workspace for compliance; retention filter configurable per customer (default 30d).
- Performance: expected additional tail latency 20–40ms for relay path under normal load; recommend burst-capacity for high-cardinality tests.
- SSO: SAML SSO for console access; service accounts for API integrations.

Open questions for Grafana team:
1) Do you want us to publish a pre-configured OTLP Source in Grafana Cloud or just provide the endpoint and schema?
2) For the Datadog co-sell flow, partner-ops asked whether we can provide a deterministic latency SLA for Dedicated tenants — we can provide guidance but need committed GPU sizing per customer to promise numbers.

I'll be available for a 30-min technical scrub if the marketplace reviewers want to go over the schema.

— Diego
Senior Integration Engineer, Redwood Inference

title: Dedicated capacity sizing: proposed slots & intake
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Evan Torres <evan@clearwave-pay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Dedicated capacity sizing: proposed slots & intake

Hi Evan —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and the concrete next steps so we can size a Dedicated pool for ClearWave Pay:

What we heard from discovery:
- Primary use: real-time payments routing + fraud scoring for API-driven flows.
- Expected peak: ~500 requests/sec (burst to 900qps for 30s) with avg payload ~1.2k tokens.
- Latency target: p95 tail < 250ms for generation/score path.
- Data handling: customer PII redaction required; will need VPC/private deployment conversation for production.

Next step: Dedicated capacity sizing call (technical deep dive) — we'll go through request patterns, token distributions, KV cache behavior, and a preliminary pool recommendation. Please confirm the throughput and latency targets above or correct them.

Proposed slots (PST):
- Wed 20 May, 10:00–11:00 AM
- Thu 21 May, 2:00–3:00 PM
- Fri 22 May, 9:00–10:00 AM

You can pick a time here: https://calendly.com/redwood/dedicated-sizing?candidate=20270520T16:00

I've attached a short intake form (dedicated-sizing-intake-form.xlsx) — filling sections A/B (traffic shape + sample prompt types) will save us 10–15 minutes on the call. Also attaching our runbook template so your infra team can preview network/kms requirements.

Agenda for the call (30–45m):
1) Quick recap of production goals and constraints
2) Walk through request distribution, sample prompts, and p95 latency targets
3) Preliminary sizing and GPU pool tradeoffs (latency vs cost)
4) Action items & mutual action plan (Doc links)

CCing Marissa (Solutions Eng) who will lead the technical deep dive. If you want anyone from infra/security on the call, please add them to your reply.

Thanks — looking forward to locking a slot.

Best,
Ben Carter
Principal Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0198

title: Kepler Commerce — Peak latency & 5xx surge: RCA, mitigations, and closure plan
preview:
From: Alex Johnson <alex@keplercommerce.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Priya Singh <priya@keplercommerce.com>
Cc: Danielle Ortiz <danielle@keplercommerce.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:12:43 -0700
Subject: Sudden spike in latency and 5xxs during Thursday peak

Ben —

We saw a sharp degradation during Thursday afternoon peak (approx 16:10–16:40 UTC): 95th pctl latency jumped from ~220ms to ~1.8s and 5xx rate climbed from baseline 0.02% to ~1.7%. This affected checkout flows and mobile app API requests. Our customers reported timeouts and cart failures for ~30 minutes.

Attached is a small excerpt of logs and a few request IDs (sample IDs: kp-7a1, kp-7a2, kp-7a3) that show repeated 503s. Can you confirm whether this is on Redwood side? We need an executive readout for our CTO and ops lead — please advise next steps and estimated ETA for root cause and mitigation.

Thanks,
Alex
Engineering Lead, Kepler Commerce
Attachments: kepler_peak_log_excerpt.csv


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Alex Johnson <alex@keplercommerce.com>
Cc: Priya Singh <priya@keplercommerce.com>, Danielle Ortiz <danielle@keplercommerce.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:34:10 -0700
Subject: Re: Sudden spike in latency and 5xxs during Thursday peak

Alex — thanks for flagging and for the sample IDs. I'm pulling ops on this now and will set up a 30-min war room to triage (joining: Karthik Iyer, Grace O'Connor). Can you share the approximate peak window in your local time and any client-side retries you observed? That helps us map traces.

Immediate asks:
- If you can forward any full request/response pairs for the sample IDs that would help.
- Screenshot of the dashboard showing error spike (if available).

I'll follow up with a short timeline once we have the initial triage. We're treating this as a Sev2 and will prioritize.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1-415-555-0182


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:50:05 -0700
Subject: Re: Sudden spike in latency and 5xxs during Thursday peak

Looping in infra/serving team. Initial triage checklist:
- Pull per-model qps/latency traces around 16:00–17:00 UTC
- Check autoscaler logs and queue build-up metrics (batching queue length)
- Verify any recent rollout or config changes to batching/kv-cache thresholds (last deploy window 05/13 02:00 UTC)
- Collect kernel-level GPU saturation metrics from the pool handling Kepler's dedicated capacity

Grace/Vivek, can you take the trace pulls and validate whether batching thresholds were hit or if we saw CPU/GPU kernel stalls? Ben, please keep Alex updated and ask for any client retry patterns.

Thanks,
Karthik
Senior Technical Program Manager — Redwood Inference


From: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 15 May 2027 14:08:22 -0700
Subject: Ops findings (initial RCA) — Kepler incident 2027-05-14

Team — summary of what ops found after trace correlation and logs:

Root cause (short):
- During the peak window, an unexpected request pattern from Kepler triggered large prefix mismatches that prevented effective KV/response caching. This led to larger-than-usual batch construction and excessive GPU kernel queueing on the dedicated pool serving Kepler.
- Concurrently, an autoscaler misconfiguration allowed one worker to fall behind (OOM-retry loop) which briefly reduced effective pool capacity. The combination produced a brief surge in p99/p95 latencies and transient 503s.

Evidence:
- Per-model traces show batching queue length increased 6x at 16:12 UTC, average tokens-per-batch rose 3x.
- GPU kernel wait times increased from median 3ms -> 58ms.
- Autoscaler logs indicate repeated container restarts on node kepler-gp-07 at 16:09–16:15 UTC.

Immediate mitigations performed (completed within 40 minutes):
- Rolled back a noncritical config change to batching threshold (rollout 05/13) to restore prior batching behavior.
- Temporarily pinned Kepler traffic to an isolated fallback pool with slightly higher head-room to reduce queuing.
- Re-provisioned node kepler-gp-07 and cleared OOM loop; autoscaler policy adjusted to prevent aggressive backoff on single node failures.

Proposed corrective actions (owners & ETA):
- Grace O'Connor: Schedule a rollout of a safer default batching profile and add automated unit test for prefix-sensitive caching rules — ETA 2027-05-26
- Vivek Kulkarni: Implement auto-detection alert when batch size or token-per-batch exceeds 4x baseline and trigger automated throttling to protect latency SLO — ETA 2027-06-03
- Karthik Iyer: Add autoscaler circuit-breaker to avoid aggressive backoff on single node — ETA 2027-05-31
- Ben Carter: Coordinate executive readout and customer credits proposal — ETA 2027-05-20

I've attached the RPM trace excerpt and a short timeline. We recommend a joint post-incident readout with Kepler's engineering and ops on 2027-05-20.

Grace O'Connor
Director, Production Reliability — Redwood Inference
Attachments: kepler_rpm_trace.json


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Alex Johnson <alex@keplercommerce.com>
Cc: Priya Singh <priya@keplercommerce.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 08:22:17 -0700
Subject: Re: Kepler Commerce — Peak latency & 5xx surge: RCA, mitigations, and closure plan

Alex — thanks again for your patience. Grace's note below contains the initial RCA and our proposed corrective actions. Proposed next steps:

1) Executive readout and Q&A: Thursday 2027-05-20 15:00 UTC (30 min) — Ben/Grace/Karthik/Vivek will attend. Please confirm who from Kepler will join (CTO + ops lead suggested).
2) Short-term remediation summary (already applied): rollback of batching change, temporary isolation to fallback pool, node reprovision.
3) Compensation: We plan to offer a one-time credit equivalent to two days' average usage for the impacted service tier, pending your approval.

I've created a short closure matrix for tracking (will update live during the readout): https://drive.google.com/kepler/ops/incident-20270514

Can you confirm availability for the readout and whether the proposed credit is acceptable? We'll circulate a final closure plan and timeline after the readout.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference

title: Procurement kickoff: ops QA, portal onboarding, and demo scheduling
Re: Quick Q about GitHub connector / can we do a short demo?
preview:
From: Liam O'Connor <liam@openhooks.dev>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Interest in GitHub connector / quick integration questions

Hey Ava,

Signed up for Redwood this morning (loving the quick onboarding — neat product!). I work on the integrations team at OpenHooks and we're evaluating a few inference providers to power a code-intent assistant we want to ship into the developer experience on GH. I had a couple quick questions before we book a demo: 

- Can the GitHub connector be scoped to specific orgs/repos and only enabled for certain teams?
- How does repo access work for private repos (PAT vs app installation)?
- Any constraints with Actions workflows (we want inference calls triggered by PR comments and workflow runs)?

Would appreciate a short 20–30m demo so we can walk through the integration pattern and security model. I'm on Pacific time and flexible most afternoons this week.

Quick bits about us: OpenHooks (YC '24) — we run a hosted service with some enterprise customers on GH Enterprise Server.

Thanks,
Liam
Maintainer, OpenHooks
liam@openhooks.dev
https://openhooks.dev


From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam@openhooks.dev>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>, Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 09:38:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Interest in GitHub connector / quick integration questions

Hi Liam — great to meet you and welcome to Redwood!

Thanks for the questions — we can absolutely scope connectors to orgs/repos and limit access by GitHub teams. For private repos we support both App installations (recommended for org-wide installs) and PAT-based approaches for one-off repos; on complex enterprise installs we usually recommend App installation + an allowlist. Actions-triggered calls are supported — we expose a tiny runtime shim you can drop into workflows so you don't ship tokens into CI.

Would love to do a 25–30 minute call to walk through the pattern and the security model. I'm available:
- Wed 19 May 10:30–11:30 PT
- Wed 19 May 15:00–16:00 PT
- Thu 20 May 13:00–15:00 PT

If none of those work, feel free to pick a time on my calendar: https://cal.redwood.ai/ava/30min

I've attached a short prep checklist that covers the pieces we'll demo (scoping, auth options, sample Actions snippet). I'll loop in Jonas (solutions) and Diego (product engineer) so we can get into specifics if you want.

Best,
Ava Chen
SDR, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: integration-prep-checklist.pdf (6KB)]

- Token rotation / audit logs for customer installs

Liam

title: Locale evaluation handoff + prioritization map
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
To: Anna Müller <anna.muller@medihealth.de>, Kenji Sato <kenji.sato@tokutech.jp>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 15 May 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Locale evaluation handoff + prioritization map

Hi Anna and Kenji,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — great conversation and clear use cases on automated triage + localized patient-facing summaries. Per our discovery, I wanted to put a short handoff note and a proposed prioritization map so we can align internal teams and get the evaluation sprint kicked off.

Key takeaways from our call:
- Primary locales: German (DE), French (FR) for EU regulated patients; Japanese (JA) for the APAC pilot.
- Main success metrics: F1 on entity extraction (clinical terms), BLEU/ROUGE for summary fidelity, and a qualitative user-acceptance score from 100 sampled summaries per locale.
- Constraints: Data residency for EU PHI-like fields, latency target ~ <350ms p50 for 512-token responses, and cost sensitivity for high-volume segments.

Recommended initial model candidates (for evaluation):
- rw-3b-ft-l10n — balanced for EU languages, good hallucination controls in our tests.
- rwi-ja-finetuned — Japanese-focused finetune; lower perplexity on clinical prompts.
- Redwood-Open-XL-quant — lower cost option; expect some regression on long-context clinical extraction without prompt engineering.

Attached: medihealth-prompt-set-v1.csv (example prompts + expected labels), benchmark-summary-eu-apac.pdf (quick read).

Proposed next steps:
1) You confirm priority locales and sample data access (S3 link or DropBox).
2) Redwood to run a 3-week pilot focusing on DE + JA (10k prompt pairs each), deliver benchmark dashboard and prompt set tuning.
3) Agree on mutual action plan and SOW + security checklist.

Can you confirm (a) priority locales, and (b) whether we can get a sanitized sample extract by EOD next Tuesday?

Thanks,

Camila Reyes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: medihealth-prompt-set-v1.csv (CSV, 38KB)]
[Attachment: benchmark-summary-eu-apac.pdf (PDF, 420KB)]

From: Anna Müller <anna.muller@medihealth.de>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>, Kenji Sato <kenji.sato@tokutech.jp>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 16 May 2027 09:03:00 +0200
Subject: Re: Locale evaluation handoff + prioritization map

Thanks, Camila — very clear summary.

Confirming priority: DE is top, FR is second, JA is pilot for APAC but lower initial scope.

A couple of immediate questions from our compliance team:
- Data residency: for the pilot we need assurance that EU PHI-like elements will not leave eu-central. Can the pilot run on Dedicated in eu-central or a Private control plane?
- Latency: our target is p50 < 350ms for 512 tokens — can you share the reference profile you quoted?

I've attached a short security questionnaire; please have your security engineer provide the highlighted items.

Also, please confirm rough cost guidance for the three-week test run (order-of-magnitude). Kenji and I will discuss internally but want to set expectations.

Best,
Anna Müller
Head of Digital Products | MediHealth GmbH
[Attachment: security-questionnaire-medihealth.docx (DOCX, 22KB)]

From: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Anna Müller <anna.muller@medihealth.de>, Kenji Sato <kenji.sato@tokutech.jp>
Date: Sun, 16 May 2027 13:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Locale evaluation handoff + prioritization map

Looping in technical recommendations and minimal risk mitigations.

Camila — thanks for the recap. A few pragmatic notes for the evaluation plan so we don't hit common regressions mid-sprint:

Evaluation design suggestions:
- Dataset size: aim for 10k prompt/expected-output pairs per prioritized locale (DE, then FR; run JA as a focused 3k mini-pilot).
- Models: use rw-3b-ft-l10n for DE/FR; rwi-ja-finetuned for JA. Keep Redwood-Open-XL-quant as a cost-control baseline, but run it separately to avoid conflating failure modes.
- Metrics: automated extraction F1, summary ROUGE-L, and a human spot-check on 300 items per locale for hallucination rate.
- Regression watch: for quantized variants, monitor token-level perplexity and named-entity omission — quantization can reduce recall on long entities.

Operational notes:
- Data residency: we can run the evaluation on Dedicated capacity in eu-central or as a Private (VPC) deployment depending on how MediHealth wants data handled. Both options available; Dedicated is quicker to stand up.
- Access: we need sanitized sample S3 or secure Share link with 3 example patients per category to do initial prompt tuning.

I created a skeleton mutual action plan and a benchmarking dashboard link: https://dash.redwood.ai/benchmarks/medihealth-eu-apac — it includes baseline runs we can extend.

Proposed cadence:
- 2027-05-24: Technical kickoff (30m) to confirm dataset paths and SOW items.
- 2027-05-25 -> 2027-06-14: 3-week evaluation window (DE primary, JA mini-pilot).
- 2027-06-18: Readout and recommended model selection.

Ben — can you prepare a cost-estimate note (ballpark) for the pilot using Dedicated eu-central?

-Neha
Neha Kapoor
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
[Link: https://drive.redwood.ai/shared/medihealth/skeleton-map.xlsx]

From: Kenji Sato <kenji.sato@tokutech.jp>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
Cc: Anna Müller <anna.muller@medihealth.de>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 16:45:00 +0900
Subject: Re: Locale evaluation handoff + prioritization map

Camila — this looks good to us.

We confirm DE as highest priority, JA as the APAC pilot. MediHealth will provide a sanitized extract by next Tuesday.

Please send a DocuSign for the mutual action plan / SOW so our procurement can fast-track approvals. Also propose a 30-minute technical kickoff next Wed (2027-05-24) at 09:00 CET / 17:00 JST.

Kenji Sato
Head of Engineering | TokuTech (partner on APAC integration)

title: Geo failover SLO carveout request — Quantum Retail
preview:
From: Priya Shah <priya@quantumretail.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@quantumretail.com>, Dan Moore <dan.moore@quantumretail.com>
Date: Wed, 12 May 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Clarify geo-failover SLA / RTO/RPO expectations

Hi Ben —

Quick question as we tighten up procurement: can Redwood confirm the SLA behavior during a full-region outage (e.g., us-east-1)? Specifically we need: 
- Target RTO for automatic failover (our internal target is <= 5 minutes)
- Target RPO (we'd like <= 15 minutes)
- Whether credits are applied for cross-region outages vs single-region degradation

Our security team is asking for the exact language that will go into the contract. If you have an ops diagram showing routing and cache warm-up assumptions that would help.

Thanks — Priya
Priya Shah
Head of Platform Ops, Quantum Retail
priya@quantumretail.com
(555) 555-0123



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Priya Shah <priya@quantumretail.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 12 May 2027 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Clarify geo-failover SLA / RTO/RPO expectations

Hi Priya —

Thanks for the clear ask. Short answer up front: we can commit to a cross-region continuity SLO that reflects operational realities, but we cannot guarantee sub-5-minute automatic failover in all scenarios without an implementation-specific runbook and additional reserved capacity.

High-level proposal (attached: redwood_sla-draft.pdf + dr-architecture-diagram.png):
- Regional uptime: 99.95% per-region availability for production endpoints.
- Cross-region failover SLO: target RTO 15 minutes (time to route + warm caches + restore KV state for active sessions).
- Cross-region recovery point: RPO target 30 minutes for non-critical logs; for token-level KV/cache we provide best-effort prefix/KV caching with eventual consistency guarantees.
- Credits: we propose a differentiated credit schedule — regional outage credits for single-region loss; separate credit tier for full-region failover events.

If Quantum requires hard 5-minute RTO / 15-minute RPO for cross-region failover, that is an accelerated SLA posture that will require dedicated warm standby capacity and a higher committed spend (we can show cost delta).

Next steps: I can loop in Marcus (runtime) and Laura (legal) to put contract language together. Also happy to run a tabletop DR exercise with your ops team.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 415 555 0199

Attachments: redwood_sla-draft.pdf (SLA language + credit schedule), dr-architecture-diagram.png


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 13 May 2027 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Clarify geo-failover SLA / RTO/RPO expectations

Ben / Monica —

Reviewed Ben's proposed carveout. A few legal/operational edits we should make before sending to Procurement: 
1) Define "region outage" precisely (carrier outage, AZ-level, control plane vs data plane).
2) Spell out the conditions for invoking cross-region credits (must be a service-affecting event determined by Redwood ops).
3) Add a 90-day run-in period for any accelerated RTO commitments and an opt-out clause for extraordinary force majeure.
4) Insert references to our SOC2 report and annual penetration test (I recommend attaching SOC2-report-2026.pdf).

Marcus — can you add a short paragraph describing cache warm-up assumptions and what customers would see during failover (e.g., ephemeral latency spike, 502s for inflight connections vs queued requests)? A one-page ops note will speed Procurement review.

Laura Bennett
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com



From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Clarify geo-failover SLA / RTO/RPO expectations

On Thu, May 13, 2027 at 09:30 AM Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com> wrote:
> Marcus — can you add a short paragraph describing cache warm-up assumptions...

Attached a short ops note and diagram that matches the draft SLA (dr-architecture-diagram.png). Key points for Procurement: 
- During a full-region failover we initiate automated DNS failover + control-plane routing to standby region. DNS TTLs, client-side caching, and active TCP connections mean observable cutover time varies between 30s-12min depending on client behavior.
- We maintain async KV replication with 1-15 minute lag under normal load; worst-case backed-off writes during region-level event can drive RPO up to 30 minutes for non-idempotent operations.
- We provide an optional warmed standby (hot-standby) for customers who want sub-5-minute RTO; this is scoped as an add-on with reserved capacity and health-check requirements.

I'll also add explicit operational triggers to the draft SLA (what constitutes "failing over to standby", who declares it, and how credits are computed).

— Marcus
Marcus Lin
Director, Serving Runtime, Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com


From: Priya Shah <priya@quantumretail.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Dan Moore <dan.moore@quantumretail.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@quantumretail.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Clarify geo-failover SLA / RTO/RPO expectations

Ben —

Thanks for the clarity and the draft. We're OK accepting the cross-region SLO: RTO 15 minutes / RPO 30 minutes as long as the contract contains: 
- Clear credit schedule for cross-region events (we want a minimum of 20% credit for an event meeting the failover threshold)
- A commitment to run an annual DR tabletop with our ops+Redwood infra (scheduling to be agreed)
- SOC2 evidence (I see SOC2-report-2026.pdf attached) and a short runbook describing what we should expect during failover (Marcus' note looks good).

Our CISO (Dan) wants DocuSign routing for SLA exhibits and a one-page summary that procurement can attach to the PO. Can you route the final exhibit via DocuSign and include the HubSpot deal id so our procurement team can reconcile?

If you can get legal to finalize and push via DocuSign by end of next week, procurement will start the PO.

— Priya
Priya Shah
Head of Platform Ops, Quantum Retail

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 17 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Forecast snaglist: pre-board resolution & narrative asks

Team —

We're T-10 business days from the board pack deadline. I pulled a quick snaglist from the ARR/bookings ledger and the forecast tab; below are the items that block a clean pack. Please treat these as blockers (not nice-to-haves).

1) Mainline renewal (DEAL-9876) - currently staged as "Commit" in HS but legal still has last SOW redline outstanding. Need product signoff and final SOW uploaded to Drive by EOD Wed so we can show Q3 bookings in the board appendix.

2) 3 mid-market deals (totaling $420k ARR) - stage is inconsistent between AE notes and CRM (two at "Proposal", one at "Verbal"). Require stage + close-date reconciliation; attach one-line notes for each.

3) Discount/credits line item - finance flagged a variance vs. last quarter's policy. Vanessa, can you confirm if the credits applied to XYZ Corp are intentional and record the approval thread?

4) Narrative bullet for ARR movement - we need a one-paragraph explanation for the net-new vs. churn delta for the board memo. Keep it <150 words, with a callout for any outliers.

Attachments: I dropped an extract (Q2_board_pack_forecast_snaglist.xlsx) into the board prep folder with tabs per item. Link in related_links.

Asks (owners & due dates):
- Mainline SOW upload + legal signoff - Marissa - Wed 18 May EOD PT
- Stage reconciliation (3 deals) - AEs, collate in the sheet - Tue 18 May noon PT (I will QA)
- Credits approval thread capture - Vanessa - Tue 18 May 3pm PT
- ARR narrative paragraph - Vivek - Wed 18 May 11am PT

Short replies OK - flag any escalation requests. We must freeze the pack by Fri COB next week.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter | Sr. Revenue Operations Manager
Redwood Inference

- On the three mid-market deals, if any AE marks "Verbal" we need corroboration via email thread (customer OK) or a signed PO. Otherwise treat as upside.

Marissa - can you prioritize the Mainline SOW redline? If legal needs a final pass, we may need a 24hr legal expedite. If you can't get it by Wed EOD, the conservative approach is to table Q3 bookings and mention the timing risk in the board note.

I'll draft the 120-word ARR narrative and share by Wed 11am PT.

Vivek

title: Hosted API partial-request failures — status entry & customer action plan
preview:
From: Elena Cruz <elena@lilypad.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: support@lilypad.ai
Date: 2027-05-17T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected failures hitting API v1 - sample IDs attached

Hi Ben,

We started seeing a spike in partial failures to your hosted inference endpoint (api-v1.redwood.com/v1/generate) around 09:05 PDT. Rough sample: 20% of requests return truncated or empty payloads with a 200 + body {"error":"partial_result"} or occasionally 502 when we retry.

Examples (timestamps are PDT):
- request_id: 7b3f-11e0-9a23, 2027-05-17T09:06:13Z, prompt length 3200 tokens
- request_id: 7b3f-11e1-0b47, 2027-05-17T09:07:02Z, prompt length 6400 tokens

We've attached a short log snippet with full headers and the response bodies (lilypad_error_snippet.txt). Can you confirm whether this is a platform-side issue or if there's something we can tune on our side? Our SLAs are impacted and customers are seeing degraded UX.

We appreciate a status page link and an ETA.

Thanks,
Elena Cruz
Head of Platform — Lilypad AI
elena@lilypad.ai



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Elena Cruz <elena@lilypad.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, support@lilypad.ai
Date: 2027-05-17T09:28:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected failures hitting API v1 - sample IDs attached

Hi Elena — thanks for the heads up and the sample IDs. We’re seeing a correlated signal in production and I’ve opened an incident on our status page: https://status.redwood.in/incidents/INC-20270517 (snapshot attached).

Immediate next steps (30m cadence updates):
- SRE (cc Marissa) is investigating root cause and running mitigations now.
- We’re routing affected traffic to a dedicated backfill pool to reduce impact on your requests.
- If you can, please temporarily limit max_tokens to 1k or pin to the compact model until we confirm resolution — we’ll clarify whether that’s necessary after triage.

Marissa — can you confirm preliminary findings and what we have running as mitigations? Jordan — looping you for customer comms/credits if needed.

Apologies for the disruption. We’ll keep updates coming every ~30 minutes on the incident page and via email.

—
Ben Carter
Director, Sales Engineering | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com
Phone: +1 415-555-0123

Attachment: statuspage-incident-snapshot.png



From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Elena Cruz <elena@lilypad.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, support@lilypad.ai
Date: 2027-05-17T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected failures hitting API v1 - sample IDs attached

Ben / Elena — preliminary technical update: 

Short summary: We observed transient kernel-level memory pressure on a subset of GPU hosts running the v1 serving stack when handling long sequences (>~4k tokens). That pressure caused partial request handling paths to return a short-circuited response for in-flight connections (manifesting as the partial_result bodies) and in rare cases a 502 when the rpc layer failed retries.

What we did (mitigations enacted 09:22–09:55 PDT):
- Enabled host spill (memory offload) for the affected pool to avoid kernel OOMs.
- Activated route-splitting to direct customers with long-sequence profiles to the dedicated cpu-backed fallback pool while we stabilize the GPU hosts.
- Increased the serving kernel’s robustness timeout and disabled one experimental kernel optimization flagged in the build for 2027-05-17.

Impact: ~18–25% of requests for sequences >4k tokens in the affected cluster experienced partial outputs between 09:05–09:40; after mitigations that dropped to ~2–3% and then to baseline by 10:00. Full clearance observed at 09:58.

Temporary customer recommendations:
- If feasible, set max_tokens <= 1000 or pin to model \"redwood-compact-v1\" until we confirm the permanent fix.
- If you need us to do the pinning/routing on your behalf we can apply it per-account (Jordan to coordinate).

We’ve attached a short orchestration log snippet showing the route split events (orchestration-log-snippet.txt). We will publish a fuller postmortem and timeline.

Marissa Cole
SRE Lead, Runtime
marissa@redwood.com

> On 2027-05-17 at 09:28, Ben Carter wrote:
> > Marissa — can you confirm preliminary findings and what we have running as mitigations?
> >
> > —
> > Ben



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Elena Cruz <elena@lilypad.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, support@lilypad.ai
Date: 2027-05-18T14:55:00-07:00
Subject: Post-incident summary and next steps — INC-20270517

Elena — thank you again for the quick report and for the logs. Quick summary and next steps from Redwood: 

What happened: transient kernel memory pressure on a subset of GPU hosts handling long sequences caused partial-response behavior for a portion of v1 requests between 09:05–09:58 PDT on 2027-05-17.

What we fixed:
- Immediate: host spill and route-splitting mitigations reduced impact and restored baseline service by 09:58.
- Short-term: we reverted an experimental kernel optimization and increased timeouts for the next 72 hours.
- Planned: a targeted kernel patch and an automated detection rule for sequence-length-driven kernel pressure (deploy ETA: 2027-05-25).

Customer impact: Lilypad AI saw request-level partial-results for ~20% of >4k-token requests during the window. We’re proposing a one-time service credit for the incident window. Jordan — can you confirm the credit amount and coordinate with Elena?

Attached: postmortem-draft.docx (internal draft). Public postmortem will be published on the incident page once we finalize language and remediation dates.

Would you be open to a short 30-minute call tomorrow to review the remediation plan and any account-specific routing we can apply? Proposed times: 09:00 PDT or 11:00 PDT — let me know what works.

Again, apologies for the disruption. We’ll continue to monitor and will notify you of the kernel patch deployment date.

—
Ben Carter
Director, Sales Engineering | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com
Phone: +1 415-555-0123

Attachments: postmortem-draft.docx

title: Cross-region BYO-Key: rekey/replication attestation request
preview:
From: Wayne Hill <wayne.hill@allegrobank.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 17 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Cross-region BYO-Key: rekey/replication attestation request

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Allegro's compliance team needs a short packet demonstrating how Redwood handles customer-provided keys for cross-region replication and specifically: 

- The HSM attestation used to protect KEK in region-to-region replica flows
- A sample rekey/rotation audit log for the last 90 days (proof of scheduled rotations + operator approvals)
- An export of the handshake manifest showing key wrapping metadata for a tenant BYOK flow (redacted where appropriate)
- Confirmation that backups stored in the secondary region are wrapped with the same KEK lineage and how emergency rekey is applied during failover

Target: we need the packet for Allegro's SOC 2 reviewer by EOW (Friday). If you can point to an S3 path or share a Drive folder with artifacts and a short narrative, that should do.

Also, please let me know who your ops contact will be — they may have follow-up questions about timestamps and operator IDs.

Best,
Wayne
Wayne Hill
Senior Security Engineer, Allegro Bank
wayne.hill@allegrobank.com

---
Attachment: request-checklist.txt (text/plain)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Wayne Hill <wayne.hill@allegrobank.com>
Date: Mon, 17 May 2027 11:03:22 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Cross-region BYO-Key: rekey/replication attestation request

Looping you both in — Allegro (Wayne) needs a compact evidence packet for SOC 2 review: HSM attestation, rekey audit logs, handshake manifest, and backup wrapping/replication notes.

Marissa — can you assemble the artifacts and redact PII where needed? Priorities:
1) HSM attestation PDF (platform-level, include cert thumbprint)
2) Rekey audit slice (2027-02 through 2027-04) with operator IDs and approval records
3) BYOK handshake manifest export (tenant-level, strip tenant secrets)
4) Replication playbook blurb for failover rekey behavior

Jonas — please confirm the attestation chain and whether we have a separate hardware root-of-trust file for cross-region wrapping (if so, include).

Wayne — is redaction to remove tenant IDs and full key IDs acceptable, or do you need the raw material under an NDA?

Thanks — aiming to deliver a drive link by Thursday EOD.

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
Phone: +1 415-555-0147

---


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Wayne Hill <wayne.hill@allegrobank.com>
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 08:45:37 -0700
Subject: Re: Cross-region BYO-Key: rekey/replication attestation request

Ben / Jonas / Wayne —

We're preparing the packet. Preliminary list of deliverables (we'll upload to the Drive folder and share a link):

- redwood_byo_handshake_manifest.csv — exported handshake records for a sample tenant (key wrap algorithm, wrap key thumbprint, region origin, replica region)
- rekey_audit_202702-202704.log — plaintext audit extract: rotation events, operator_id (internal id), approval token id, UTC timestamp. We'll redact tenant GUIDs and wrap key material.
- replication-kek-rotation-schedule.xlsx — our scheduled rotation cadence and the automated promotion rules used during cross-region failover
- draft narrative: \"Cross-region BYOK handling and emergency rekey\" (short doc included as plain text)

We will NOT include private key material. All artifacts will be read-only links. Expected upload ETA: today by 5pm PT.
