title: Overlap in maintenance attribution & probe fingerprint mismatch
preview:
From: Samantha Ruiz <samantha@cloudwave.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>
Cc: cloudwave-ops@cloudwave.com
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Observed uptime discrepancy during 05/12 maintenance window

Jonas,

We ran our reconciliation for May and noticed that the availability ledger shows a 0.9% uptime hit attributed to Redwood for the 05/12 03:00-04:30 UTC maintenance window. Our records indicate our maintenance window was 03:00-03:45 and the downstream provider (EdgeRoute) had a 03:40-04:00 maintenance — there appears to be an overlap that your calculation attributed fully to Redwood.

Can you share the exact attribution rules you used? In particular, our probe traces show traffic recovered at 03:46 but a series of synthetic probes timed out again at 04:15 — we're concerned those later timeouts were caused by EdgeRoute's concurrent change and not Redwood.

We need this reconciled ahead of our monthly review on Monday. Attaching our probe dump and a short summary.

—Samantha
Samantha Ruiz
Head of SRE, CloudWave
attachments: cvp_probe_log_20270514.csv, summary-May-recon.pdf

From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>
To: Samantha Ruiz <samantha@cloudwave.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 10:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Observed uptime discrepancy during 05/12 maintenance window

Hi Samantha — thanks for flagging, and for the probe dump. We're looking into the timeline now. A couple quick notes:

- Our standard attribution logic treats any customer-facing unavailability within a scheduled maintenance window as maintenance-excluded if it falls entirely within the customer-declared maintenance window.
- For overlaps we apportion based on first-failure timestamps and probe fanout weighting; however, we also apply a fingerprint heuristic to map failures to provider change signatures (config diffs, BGP flaps, TLS handshake errors, etc.).

To move fast, can you confirm whether the EdgeRoute maintenance was declared to you (CloudWave) ahead of time and whether you saw any config diffs in the 04:10-04:30 range? I'm attaching our preliminary query results (gateway_config_diff.txt) and will run a cross-probe correlation.

Jonas Weber
Director, Customer Success — Redwood Inference
attachments: gateway_config_diff.txt

From: cloudwave-ops@cloudwave.com
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 15 May 2027 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Observed uptime discrepancy during 05/12 maintenance window

Jonas/Marissa,

EdgeRoute posted a brief maintenance notice to their portal (ticket ER-2205) at 03:35 UTC — not directly to us — and we only saw the downstream config drift at ~04:12 (we've included the packet-level sample). We don't have visibility into their early stage routing tweak at 03:40.

We agree that the 03:00-03:45 window is ours. The additional timeouts at ~04:12 appear correlated to EdgeRoute's change, but several probes that hit Redwood showed TLS handshake errors rather than route drops. We're happy to let you run your fingerprinting heuristic, but our ask is that Redwood does not attribute the entire 03:00-04:30 block to our maintenance.

—CloudWave Ops

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Samantha Ruiz <samantha@cloudwave.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 17 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Observed uptime discrepancy during 05/12 maintenance window

Samantha — thanks for the context and for the packet sample. We've completed a manual reconciliation:

Summary of findings:
- 03:00–03:45 UTC: confirmed CloudWave-declared maintenance window; Redwood excludes this period from availability calculations.
- 03:40–03:59 UTC: EdgeRoute initial change detected; Redwood's probes show route-level packet loss but TLS handshakes largely succeeded after failover; we apportion 03:40–03:59 as shared responsibility (50/50) because both maintenance sequences were active.
- 04:00–04:30 UTC: two distinct clusters of failures at ~04:12 and ~04:19 that align with EdgeRoute's config change propagation; probe payloads indicate upstream BGP churn and not Redwood-side service failures. We therefore attribute 04:00–04:30 to EdgeRoute/third-party and exclude it from Redwood's uptime tally.

Action items we propose:
1) Apply a re-apportionment so Redwood's ledger removes the full 04:00–04:30 window and splits 03:40–03:59 50/50.
2) Share an amended availability report and updated tokenized ledger entry for May by EOD Tuesday.
3) Add the EdgeRoute change signature to our fingerprint library so future overlaps are classified deterministically.

I have attached the cross-probe correlation and the gateway config diff we used. If that approach sounds good, we'll implement and update the ledger. If not, call me and we can go over the packet-level traces together.

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Sr. Manager, Customer Reliability — Redwood Inference
attachments: cross_probe_correlation_20270514.csv (see drive link), gateway_config_diff.txt

Our records indicate our maintenance window was 03:00-03:45 and the downstream provider (EdgeRoute) had a 03:40-04:00 maintenance — there appears to be an overlap that your calculation attributed fully to Redwood.

Subject: Re: Observed uptime discrepancy during 05/12 maintenance window

Hi Samantha — thanks for flagging, and for the probe dump. We're looking into the timeline now. A couple quick notes:

- Our standard attribution logic treats any customer-facing unavailability within a scheduled maintenance window as maintenance-excluded if it falls entirely within the customer-declared maintenance window.
- For overlaps we apportion based on first-failure timestamps and probe fanout weighting; however, we also apply a fingerprint heuristic to map failures to provider change signatures (config diffs, BGP flaps, TLS handshake errors, etc.).

To move fast, can you confirm whether the EdgeRoute maintenance was declared to you (CloudWave) ahead of time and whether you saw any config diffs in the 04:10-04:30 range? I'm attaching our preliminary query results (gateway_config_diff.txt) and will run a cross-probe correlation.

title: SignalWorks POC: credit escrow and pricing path
preview:
From: Alyssa Greene <alyssa@signalworks.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Hsu <t.hsu@signalworks.com>
Date: 2027-05-09T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Quick: POC timeline, credits, and pricing path

Hi Ben —

Thanks again for the demo last week. SignalWorks' execs want a clean path to production if the POC shows the expected latency/cost wins. Two asks from our side:

1) Can we get a POC credit top-up (we're almost through the current tranche) for an extra two weeks while we finish the last integration?
2) We need a clear conversion path (discount ladder and commitment tiers) baked into the MAP so procurement can sign off quickly.

Can you share a simple MAP that ties milestones to credits and final pricing? If possible, include an escrow or holdback approach so we can close faster.

Alyssa
Alyssa Greene
VP, Platform Engineering | SignalWorks
[Attachment: initial_poc_report.pdf (pdf, 120KB)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alyssa Greene <alyssa@signalworks.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>, Tom Hsu <t.hsu@signalworks.com>
Date: 2027-05-10T10:11:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Quick: POC timeline, credits, and pricing path

Alyssa —

Appreciate the context. I put together a short MAP draft that does three things:
- Extends POC by 14 days with a one-time credit top-up (we can apply up to $12k of credits, staged by milestone).
- Defines three acceptance milestones (latency, throughput, accuracy) with a credit escrow that releases on acceptance.
- Shows a conversion ladder: 3-month commit (baseline price), 6-month (5% discount), 12-month (12% discount) with an onboarding credit applied against first invoice.

I'm attaching the MAP v3 and a PO template you can use. If procurement prefers an escrow escrow managed by us (credit holdback) vs. a purchase order hold, we can do either — happy to outline both flows.

Next step: Priyom will propose milestone definitions and acceptance tests. Lauren will confirm commercial guardrails if we need an exception to standard discount caps.

Thanks,
Ben
Senior Account Exec | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: MAP_SignalWorks_v3.xlsx (xlsx, 45KB)]

From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
To: Alyssa Greene <alyssa@signalworks.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-12T08:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: RE: Quick: POC timeline, credits, and pricing path

Alyssa / Ben —

Draft milestone language below (short, measurable):

Milestone 1 — Integration smoke tests: 3 days after access, pass automated prompt throughput (>= 150 rps for 512-token average) and logging integration. Release: 30% of POC credit top-up.

Milestone 2 — Performance validation: end of week 2, 95th percentile latency under 250ms for demo scenario and model accuracy within agreed evaluation set. Release: 40% of credit top-up.

Milestone 3 — Ramp / stability: one week of steady-state traffic at target QPS (customer UAT). Release: final 30% and onboarding credit applied toward conversion invoice.

Escrow flow: Redwood holds the POC credit in customer account; credits are consumed against usage and any remaining holdback is credited to first paid invoice if customer converts within 60 days.

If SignalWorks procurement wants a PO-based prepay instead, we can mirror the holdback by invoicing and holding a portion until acceptance.

I'll attach the evaluation checklist and a sample telemetry payload.

- Priyom
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: POC_telemetry_sample.json (json, 8KB)]

From: Alyssa Greene <alyssa@signalworks.com>
To: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@signalworks.com, Tom Hsu <t.hsu@signalworks.com>
Date: 2027-05-13T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Re: RE: Quick: POC timeline, credits, and pricing path

Thanks Priyom — this is exactly what I needed. Two quick clarifications from procurement: 

1) Instead of credits being consumed automatically, procurement asks for a $6k escrow line item (prepay) that we can invoice against; any unused escrow transfers to the first paid invoice on conversion.
2) We need a guaranteed conversion price cap for the first 12 months (so execs can model spend). Can you add a capped rate per 1M tokens and the discount tiers in the MAP?

If you can send an updated MAP with the escrow wording and a simple table of rate caps, procurement is ready to route for approval.

Alyssa
VP, Platform Engineering | SignalWorks

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Alyssa Greene <alyssa@signalworks.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-15T14:20:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Re: Re: RE: Quick: POC timeline, credits, and pricing path

Alyssa —

Quick note from commercial leadership: we can support the $6k escrow prepay and map it to the POC credit top-up. On conversion we will apply any remaining escrow as a credit to the first invoice. Attached is the rate-cap table for the 12-month window:
- Baseline (month-to-month): $22 / 1M tokens
- 3-month commit: $20 / 1M tokens
- 6-month commit: $19 / 1M tokens (5% off baseline)
- 12-month commit: $19.36 / 1M tokens (12% off baseline) — note: final discount conditioned on annualized commit >= threshold in MAP.

We will honor the rate cap for 12 months from the conversion effective date. I put a brief clause about force-majeure and model architecture drift that preserves Redwood's right to re-evaluate price if the customer's token mix changes materially.

Ben: please confirm procurement flow (invoice PO vs credit hold) and attach final MAP + PO template.

Thanks,
Lauren Bishop
Head of Commercial | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Rate_Cap_Table.pdf (pdf, 12KB)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alyssa Greene <alyssa@signalworks.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-17T15:44:00-07:00
Subject: Final: MAP + escrow flow and next steps

Alyssa —

Attaching the finalized MAP (MAP_SignalWorks_v3.xlsx) and the PO template (PO_Template_SignalWorks.docx). Summary of the agreed commercial flow: 

- SignalWorks issues a $6,000 PO (escrow/prepay) to cover the POC top-up.
- Redwood will provision an additional $12,000 in usage credit; the $6k prepay covers half and Redwood carries the remaining $6k as a contingent credit (released per milestone).
- Acceptance milestones as defined by Priyom will trigger release of the contingent credit and apply remaining prepay to invoices.
- Conversion pricing will be capped per Lauren's table for 12 months post-conversion.

Next actions:
1) Alyssa: send PO to procurement@redwood.com (we accept standard PO or DocuSign SOW).
2) Marissa (CS) will schedule onboarding call for Monday 5/24 to kick off integration tests.

If that matches your understanding, I'll route the final MAP for signatures and mark the HubSpot deal as "Commit: POC Escrow Accepted."

Ben
Senior Account Exec | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: MAP_SignalWorks_v3.xlsx (xlsx, 45KB); PO_Template_SignalWorks.docx (docx, 28KB)]

- Tue May 18 — 11:00–12:00
- Wed May 19 — 09:00–10:00
- Thu May 20 — 15:00–16:00

Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 15:02:00 -0400
Subject: Re: MSA redline handoff — proposed slots

Hi Jordan — thanks for sending this over and for the summary.

We usually run a 45–60 minute redline handoff but to avoid a single point-of-failure on scheduling I wanted to propose a \"slot-pool\" approach: provide 3 adjacent half-hour windows over two days and we pick the one that best suits cross-team availability. That tends to reduce back-and-forth when legal or engineering needs to swap.

Our available slot-pool (ET):
- Mon May 17 — 10:30–11:15 or 11:30–12:15 or 13:00–13:45
- Tue May 18 — 09:00–09:45 or 10:00–10:45 or 11:00–11:45

Required attendees: Aventis legal + procurement, Redwood legal (Laura) and CS (Ben) for onboarding checkpoints.

I've attached our working draft of the redline (MSA_aventis_redline_v1.docx) and an internal onboarding checklist so we can surface any pre-call asks. If you confirm a preferred pool/time, we will send a calendar invite and agenda.

Thanks,
Aisha Rahman
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference

title: Privacy roadmap: DPA intake, SCC module check, controller/processor clarification
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marta Kovac <marta.kovac@fidelityhealth.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 14:10:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo recap + next steps on DPA/SCC questions

Hi Marta — great to meet you and Igor on the call today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can keep momentum for the procurement/legal review at FidelityHealth.

Summary (short):
- Redwood is happy to support FidelityHealth as a data controller for customer-provided data. We recognize the importance of clarifying roles before formalizing a DPA.
- You asked about SCC module preference and whether the standard EU SCCs or the new modular approach is acceptable for your legal team.
- SOC 2 status: we shared that Redwood has a rolling Type II audit in progress; I’ve called out timing below.

Questions/action items we need from you:
1) Confirm whether FidelityHealth will be signing as Controller for the product (it sounded like yes on today’s call).
2) Please confirm whether your legal team has a strong preference for the SCC module flavor (we can provide both Module 2/Module 3 mapping guidance).
3) Security contact for the vendor security questionnaire (DSQ) so we can kick off scoping and deliver the initial answers in 3 business days.

What I’m attaching here: Redwood DPA template (editable), a short SCC variant matrix to show typical mapping, and a scope worksheet for the DSQ. If your legal prefers to mark up our DPA, we can review and follow with a DocuSign-ready agreement.

Proposed next steps:
- You (or legal) confirm controller/processor roles and SCC module preference (reply to this thread).
- We will populate the DSQ and send a first-pass response + evidence links within 72 hours.
- Redwood legal to prepare DocuSign link for the DPA once your counsel has marked up or accepted the template.

Thanks again — happy to jump on a short follow-up call with your legal or security teams if helpful.

Best,
Ava Chen
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0142

Attachments:
- redwood_dpa_template.pdf (PDF, DPA template)
- redwood_scc_matrix.csv (CSV, SCC module mapping)
- vendor_security_q_scope.xlsx (XLSX, DSQ scope worksheet)

From: Marta Kovac <marta.kovac@fidelityhealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Igor Petrov <igor.petrov@fidelityhealth.com>, FidelityHealth Legal <legal@fidelityhealth.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 17:03:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo recap + next steps on DPA/SCC questions

Hi Ava — thanks for the clear recap. A few clarifications from our side after a quick internal sync:

1) Controller/Processor: FidelityHealth will be signing as Controller for customer data we upload; Redwood will be Processor for those flows. Please include that as the starting assumption in the DPA.

2) SCC module: our legal team prefers the modular SCC approach (Module 2 where Redwood processes on our behalf), but they will want to see the mapping of subprocessors and the cross-border transfer mechanics.

3) SOC 2: we rely heavily on SOC 2 Type II reports. Can you confirm your current state and anticipated completion date for a Type II report? We need an ETA and whether any interim evidence is available now.

4) DSQ: Please start the vendor security questionnaire — our security lead Igor will own the technical review. His contact is igor.petrov@fidelityhealth.com. Send initial answers and mark any gaps.

Small ask on timing: can you get the initial DSQ answers + the DPA (marked with controller/processor roles) to us by EOW (Monday)? Our procurement kickoff is scheduled for Tuesday and having those docs will help.

Thanks — Marta
Marta Kovac
Sr. Procurement Manager | FidelityHealth
+marta.kovac@fidelityhealth.com

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marta Kovac <marta.kovac@fidelityhealth.com>
Cc: Igor Petrov <igor.petrov@fidelityhealth.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, FidelityHealth Legal <legal@fidelityhealth.com>
Date: Sat, 15 May 2027 09:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo recap + next steps on DPA/SCC questions

Thanks Marta — happy to confirm next steps and timing. Per internal alignment:

- Controller/Processor: we'll mark Redwood as Processor and FidelityHealth as Controller in the DPA template we attached.
- SCC module: attached CSV maps our standard Module 2 mapping to common contract clauses and subprocessors. If legal prefers Module 3 variants we can adapt quickly.
- SOC 2: Redwood has completed SOC 2 Type I (2025) and is currently in a rolling Type II audit cycle — expected issuance: Aug 2027. In the meantime we can provide interim evidence: our control matrix, penetration test summary (redacted), and the SOC2 audit kickoff letter. Links/attachments below.

Files we are sharing on this thread (initial package):
- initial_dsq_answers.docx (first-pass DSQ answers, marked TODOs)
- redwood_dpa_template.pdf (DPA with roles pre-marked)

- Drive folder w/ artifacts: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cRedwood-FH-Privacy

1) Neha will complete the DSQ gaps and add evidence links by EOD Monday.
2) Lauren will coordinate with my team and Redwood Legal to accept or track counsels’ marks in the DocuSign draft.
3) If Igor or your security team wants a short technical follow-up we can do a 30-minute call early next week — suggest Tue morning (PDT)/Tue afternoon (ET).

Ava

title: Cloud marketplace rebilling & commercial terms — next steps
preview:
From: Marco Rossi <marco.rossi@cloudpartner.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: contracts@cloudpartner.com
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Draft partner paper & billing flow for reseller channel

Ben — attaching the partner paper draft we discussed. Two items we want to lock before we go to legal: (1) confirmation of the billing/rebilling flow for marketplace transactions (our finance team needs to know whether you require pass-through invoicing or if CloudPartner will invoice end-customers and remit to Redwood), and (2) a short list of minimum security/compliance requirements to include in the addendum.

For context: our marketplace ops team enforces a single pass-through model by default, but some of our larger resellers ask us to accept net-of-fees payments (i.e., CloudPartner invoices, Redwood invoices our portion). Curious what works for you.

Attachments: CloudPartner_PartnerPaper_Draft.pdf

—Marco
Marco Rossi
Head of Partnerships, CloudPartner Inc
marco.rossi@cloudpartner.com
+1 (415) 555-0182


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marco Rossi <marco.rossi@cloudpartner.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 11:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft partner paper & billing flow for reseller channel

Thanks Marco — sharing with our legal and ops leads so we can get a clean ask back to you. Quick notes from sales perspective:
- We prefer the pass-through model for Marketplace listings (Redwood receives a pass-through invoice showing gross usage, CloudPartner remits the Redwood net). That keeps our billing and usage reporting aligned for customer support and audit requests.
- If CloudPartner can’t provide itemized pass-through invoices, we need a clear reconciliation cadence and a commitment to provide token-level usage logs on request.

Karthik / Kimberly / Laura — can you review the attached draft and advise: (a) must-have redlines for MSA/order-form; (b) specific language on fee routing and auditability; (c) whether Redwood can accept a limited net-of-fees flow in exceptional enterprise cases.

Goal: return to Marco with a concise redline set and a preferred fallback by EOD Thursday so their procurement can start finalizing.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 12 May 2027 08:36:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft partner paper & billing flow for reseller channel

Thanks — reviewed the draft. I attached a redline (Redwood_Marketplace_Addendum_Redlines_v1.docx) and a 1-page change summary for exec review. High-level legal positions: 

1) Fee routing (pass-through vs net-of-fees): our preference is pass-through invoicing (CloudPartner issues invoice showing gross usage, Redwood receives separate invoice for our portion). If CloudPartner insists on net-of-fees, we can accept only when: (a) an explicit reconciliation schedule is in the addendum (monthly), (b) CloudPartner agrees to provide certified usage exports within 7 business days of Redwood request, and (c) a carve-out exists for audits by Redwood or Redwood's auditors.

2) Indemnity and liability allocation: narrow reseller carve-outs — we need language preserving Redwood's indemnity for model outputs and a limitation of liability that applies across marketplace transactions.

3) Data & auditability: require that CloudPartner provide per-customer token usage logs and a machine-readable billing export (CSV or JSON) for reconciliations. Also request the ability to request a one-time audit annually.

4) Pricing & tax flow: clarify that any taxes collected by CloudPartner are segregated and reported; Redwood needs visibility into tax line items for VAT/GST purposes in EU customers.

Ops note to Laura: will need your confirmation that the per-customer usage export format described in section 3 is operationally practical.

If we can get alignment on 1–3, we can sign the addendum with the proposed limitation language. If CloudPartner refuses pass-through entirely, we should decide whether to escalate to pricing execs for carve-out approvals.

Attachments: Redwood_Marketplace_Addendum_Redlines_v1.docx; Addendum_Change_Summary.pdf

—Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwood.com


From: Marco Rossi <marco.rossi@cloudpartner.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 13 May 2027 15:03:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Draft partner paper & billing flow for reseller channel

Appreciate the fast turnaround and clear redlines. A few clarifications from our side: 
- CloudPartner's standard marketplace ops do prefer to invoice end-customers and remit to partners (net-of-fees). Changing that to a pass-through model for all listings would require changes to our billing platform and delays to the launch schedule.
- That said, for strategic partners we can implement enhanced billing exports and a reconciliation flow that meets Redwood's audit needs; this is a configuration we can enable for the initial set of enterprise customers.

Proposal: We can accept the pass-through-style visibility without changing our core invoice flow by doing the following: (a) CloudPartner will continue to issue invoices to end-customers and remit per the partner settlement process, (b) CloudPartner will deliver a certified monthly usage file and line-item tax breakdown to Redwood, and (c) for any disputed amounts Redwood can trigger a 7-business-day reconciliation request.

We will also need Redwood to accept standard reseller carve-outs to liability where CloudPartner is acting as the merchant of record. Can you confirm which specific indemnity carve-outs you find non-negotiable?

I can put our contracts lead on a short call tomorrow if that helps close open language. Also sharing a DocuSign link for the base agreement our GC prefers for logistics: https://docusign.com/envelopes/0a1b2c3d

—Marco


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marco Rossi <marco.rossi@cloudpartner.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 15 May 2027 14:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft partner paper & billing flow for reseller channel

Thanks team — quick summary and proposed next steps so we have an action-oriented closure: 

Summary of alignment:
- CloudPartner will keep invoice flow as merchant-of-record (net-of-fees) but will provide certified, machine-readable monthly usage exports and per-line tax details to Redwood (meets legal's auditability ask).
- CloudPartner will accept a 7-business-day reconciliation trigger for disputed amounts.
- Redwood will accept limited reseller liability carve-outs where CloudPartner is acting as merchant of record, but Kimberly will draft the exact carve-out language that preserves Redwood's indemnity for model outputs.

Action items / owners:
- Kimberly: propose final carve-out + indemnity language by EOD Monday (will include clause that retains Redwood output indemnity).
- Laura: confirm operational format for usage export (CSV/JSON template) and confirm turnaround SLA by Wed.
- Karthik: schedule a 30-minute sync with CloudPartner contracts lead — aim Monday 10am PT.
- Marco/CloudPartner: confirm DocuSign logistics and whether you need Redwood to sign a BAU doc for launch.

If that sounds right, Kimberly — once you post the final language I'll loop in our pricing execs if there are any material commercial carve-outs to approve. Marco, appreciate the flexibility here — looks like a pragmatic middle ground that preserves auditability without a billing platform rewrite.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
Attachments referenced: Redwood_Marketplace_Addendum_Redlines_v1.docx, Addendum_Change_Summary.pdf

title: Urgent: Reserved pool pressure impacting MercuryPay - status and next steps
preview:
From: Ellie Park <ellie.park@mercurypay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-14T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Latency/error spike for reserved pool (MercuryPay)

Ben —

We're seeing a sustained increase in 95th/99th p99 latency and a higher error rate from calls routed to our reserved pool over the last 90 minutes. A handful of customer-facing workflows are timing out (500s + socket timeouts). We raised a ticket on our side but wanted to alert you since this is hitting production and we have a peak window this afternoon.

Can you confirm whether this is a configuration change on your side, and whether we are still on the reserved capacity we purchased (DL-4892)? We need an ETA on mitigation and any temporary workarounds (routing to hosted or lowered concurrency) you can perform.

Thanks — Ellie
Ellie Park
Director, Platform Engineering
MercuryPay

[Attachment: merc-pay-error-snippets.txt (plain text) — representative 500s and call traces]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ellie Park <ellie.park@mercurypay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-14T09:29:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Latency/error spike for reserved pool (MercuryPay)

Ellie — thanks for the heads up and the snippets. I'm escalating internally now. Short answers: you are still on your reserved capacity but we're seeing elevated queueing on the pool that is serving MercuryPay. I'm setting up a mitigation plan and will share an ETA in the next 30 minutes.

Immediate options we're considering:
- Temporarily route non-critical endpoints to hosted fallback (if you approve)
- Apply a conservative concurrency cap per-session to reduce KV cache pressure
- Add priority routing for your production tokens to reduce interference from bursty tenants

I'll loop in SRE and product to get a definitive timeline. Sorry for the disruption — we'll prioritize this.

— Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales (EMEA & US)
Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-14T09:42:00-07:00
Subject: INTERNAL: MercuryPay reserved pool pressure — telemetry + suggested actions

Notes from quick triage: we see two correlated signals in the pool that handles MercuryPay:
- Sustained increase in sequence lengths for a subset of sessions (avg seq len +70% over baseline) causing KV cache churn and memory pressure.
- Several other tenants in the same dedicated cluster started a high-concurrency experiment ~08:30 PDT, which coupled with MercuryPay's production spike produced backpressure.

Telemetry snapshot (full CSV attached): pool-telemetry-20270514.csv
Immediate suggested mitigation (short-term) — implement in this order:
1) Apply per-customer token priority to ensure MercuryPay requests preempt lower-priority bursts (will cost ~5% throughput for others).
2) Enable conservative per-session concurrency cap: 2 active reqs per session for 30 min.
3) If latency remains > p95 target after 15m, shift non-critical routes to hosted fallback.

Longer-term: we need to provision a dedicated buffer pool for large-sequence workloads and formal isolation for high-burst tenants. Recommend an emergency meeting with MercuryPay ops + Redwood SRE at 10:15 to approve fallback.

Marissa
VP, Customer Reliability
[Attachment: pool-telemetry-20270514.csv]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ellie Park <ellie.park@mercurypay.com>, MercuryPay SRE <sre@mercurypay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-14T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Latency/error spike for reserved pool (MercuryPay) — immediate steps

Ellie — short update following internal triage and an SRE runbook: we are going to perform the following now (starting 10:05 PDT):
1) Enable per-customer priority on your tokens so MercuryPay traffic preempts lower priority bursts. Expected effect: immediate drop in tail latency for your calls, minimal risk to correctness.
2) Apply a 2-request concurrency cap per session for 30 minutes to limit cache thrashing.
3) Stand up hosted fallback routes for non-critical ingestion endpoints (we will need your signoff to reroute these).

Can you confirm: which of the MercuryPay endpoints are non-critical for this 90-minute mitigation window? If you prefer, we can route all requests from non-prod tokens automatically.

I'll send a status update after the first 15 minutes.

— Ben

From: Ellie Park <ellie.park@mercurypay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: MercuryPay SRE <sre@mercurypay.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>

Date: 2027-05-14T09:42:00-07:00
Subject: INTERNAL: MercuryPay reserved pool pressure — telemetry + suggested actions

Notes from quick triage: we see two correlated signals in the pool that handles MercuryPay:
- Sustained increase in sequence lengths for a subset of sessions (avg seq len +70% over baseline) causing KV cache churn and memory pressure.
- Several other tenants in the same dedicated cluster started a high-concurrency experiment ~08:30 PDT, which coupled with MercuryPay's production spike produced backpressure.

Telemetry snapshot (full CSV attached): pool-telemetry-20270514.csv
Immediate suggested mitigation (short-term) — implement in this order:
1) Apply per-customer token priority to ensure MercuryPay requests preempt lower-priority bursts (will cost ~5% throughput for others).
2) Enable conservative per-session concurrency cap: 2 active reqs per session for 30 min.
3) If latency remains > p95 target after 15m, shift non-critical routes to hosted fallback.

Longer-term: we need to provision a dedicated buffer pool for large-sequence workloads and formal isolation for high-burst tenants. Recommend an emergency meeting with MercuryPay ops + Redwood SRE at 10:15 to approve fallback.

VP, Customer Reliability

title: Multi-tenant throttle spike — coordinated mitigation & customer note
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>, CloudPartner Solutions <support@cloudpartner.io>
Cc: Streamline Labs <ops@streamlinelabs.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Multi-tenant throttle spike — coordinated mitigation & customer note

Team —

We just saw a noticeable p95/p99 latency increase and a ~30% sustained throughput drop for a multi-tenant group in us-west-2 starting ~08:50 PDT. The initial correlation points to an aggressive kernel scheduling backoff plus cross-tenant batching interactions; Streamline Labs is one of the impacted customers (they're in prod and report user-facing errors).

Immediate asks:
- Karthik: can you triage kernel scheduling / priority-queue behaviour and confirm if we can temporarily raise Streamline's priority or isolate them on a separate worker pool?
- Marissa / CS: I need proposed customer comms (one-paragraph transparency + ETA) we can send within 30–45m.
- Evelyn: any quick guardrails on credits or SLA exceptions for this class of incident?

I'm attaching an initial spike chart and the incident playbook fragment. I'll keep this thread for coordination and will strip extenal once we have the draft comms.

[attachment: svc_metrics_20270514.png] [attachment: incident_playbook_v2.pdf]

Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com



From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudPartner Solutions <support@cloudpartner.io>, Streamline Labs <ops@streamlinelabs.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Multi-tenant throttle spike — coordinated mitigation & customer note

Ben / all —

Quick triage notes:
- We've seen an uptick in kernel-level preemption tied to a recent scheduler tweak (rolled 05/12) that interacts poorly with continuous-batching for long-context jobs.
- Root cause candidate: suboptimal priority demotion for tenants hitting token spikes, causing eviction/backpressure across the worker pool.

Short-term mitigations I can enact right now (need ops approval / iaas access):
1) Pin Streamline requests to a high-priority slice for the next 4 hours (will require a config flip; risk: increased tail latency for best-effort tenants).
2) Expand the worker pool in us-west-2 by 6 instances (estimated 18% throughput uplift; cost covered by burst budget).
3) Adjust batching window for long-context jobs to reduce cross-tenant KV pressure (temporary 10% QPS impact, but stabilizes p99).

I'll start #1 and #2 if Evelyn gives the go-ahead. I've opened a Jira with traces and kernel logs: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/INF-2345 (linked).

Karthik
Karthik Iyer
SRE Lead, Serving Runtime
karthik@redwood.com



From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Cc: Streamline Labs <ops@streamlinelabs.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Multi-tenant throttle spike — coordinated mitigation & customer note

Thanks — drafting a short note for ops@streamlinelabs. Proposed customer message below; please review for technical accuracy and tone. If we agree, Ben can send and I'll follow up with a CS playbook.

--- draft message to Streamline Labs ---
Subject: Brief service disruption and mitigation plan (May 14)

Hi Team —

We detected increased latency and reduced throughput for a subset of requests this morning in our us-west region which may have impacted your users. Our engineers have identified a scheduling interaction affecting multi-tenant request handling.

Immediate actions taken: we've moved your production traffic to a higher-priority slice and scaled additional capacity; we're also applying a temporary batching configuration to stabilize tail latency. We expect user-facing impact to be resolved within 60 minutes.

Next steps: we'll follow up with a technical post-mortem and a timeline for the permanent fix. As an apology for the disruption, we're prepared to offer a billing credit for affected hours — I'll propose details after we confirm the scope.

If you need an interim contact or status updates, reply here and we'll escalate into a call.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
--- end draft ---

Can we sign this off? Also recommend offering a 20% credit for the 2 impacted hours (preliminary).



From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudPartner Solutions <support@cloudpartner.io>, Streamline Labs <ops@streamlinelabs.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 10:05:00 -07:00

Marissa — 20% credit for the 2-hour window is approved; finance will handle the adjustment against DL-7821. Please note the credit in the customer note.

Also: log this as an SLA exception under the emergency policy and tag INF-2345. I'll coordinate a high-priority post-mortem with Eng and CS.

Evelyn Hart
Head of Customer Operations
evelyn@redwood.com

title: Atlas Retail — buying path, pilot milestones & owner commitments

Date: Wed, 12 May 2027 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Atlas Retail discovery — summary, proposed milestones & asks

Ellen / team — thanks again for the 45-minute call earlier. Quick written recap so we have an agreed starting place and owners for the next 6–8 weeks. I’ve attached a first-pass Mutual Action Plan (RWI-MAP-draft-v2.pdf) and a rough sizing estimate.

Key pain + goals we heard
- High volume of customer chat and returns-related triage taking agent time — estimated 30% of support load.
- Inconsistent answer quality from existing rule-based automations; goal is to raise first-contact resolution and reduce avg handle time.
- Compliance: logs retention and auditability for customer PII redaction.

Success metrics you called out
- Pilot: 10% reduction in agent handle time; maintain accuracy >= 92% on a sampled set.
- Post-pilot decision: Exec procurement approval by end of May (procurement timeline you mentioned).

Proposed mutual action plan (high level) — owners / dates
1) Contract + Procurement intake — Atlas Procurement (Tom/Procurement) — target: 2027-05-28 (owner: Tom Rivera)
2) Security + legal exchanges — Atlas security + Redwood Security (deliverable: security brief + questionnaire) — target: 2027-05-21 (owner: Ellen / Ben)
3) Pilot scoping & data prep — Atlas Product + Redwood Solutions (deliverable: data extract + evaluation prompts) — target: 2027-06-11 (owner: Karthik / Atlas PM)
4) Pilot run (4 weeks) — infrastructure + monitoring in Redwood Dedicated — 2027-06-21 to 2027-07-18 (owner: Redwood infra & Atlas Ops)
5) Pilot demo & results review — execs + CS — 2027-07-22 (owner: Ben / Monica)

Immediate asks from our side
- Please confirm procurement approver and timeline (Tom?) and copy to procurement@atlasretail.com.
- Share the security questionnaire items you need; we’ve attached 'redwood-security-brief.pdf' as a starter.
- Confirm Atlas PM for data prep so Karthik can schedule the scoping workshop.

Attachments: RWI-MAP-draft-v2.pdf (mutual action plan), sizing-estimate.xlsx

Next step: If this looks right, can you confirm owners for line items 1–3 by end of day Friday? We’ll lock dates and circulate a final MAP.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0119

title: Finixa trial readiness — RPS, burst behavior, SLAs & owner assignments
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Olivia Ramos <olivia@finixa.com>, Dan Li <dan.li@finixa.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 12 May 2027 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Finixa trial readiness — RPS, burst behavior, SLAs & owner assignments

Hi Olivia / Dan — thanks again for the call earlier. Quick recap and concrete asks so we can scope the trial correctly: 

1) Success criteria (please confirm):
- p99 generation latency <= 600ms for the default 95-token prompt profile
- Availability >= 99.9% during business hours (M–F 9am–5pm PT)
- Top-3 output relevance >= 92% on your evaluation set (you mentioned ~1k syn/real prompts)

2) Workload inputs we need from you (attached is a small example CSV):
- Typical RPS (avg) and peak RPS (burst) — values and the duration of bursts (e.g., 45s at 120 RPS)
- Concurrency targets (clients / sessions concurrently holding KV caches)
- Token distributions (prompt, generation avg and 95th percentile)
- Any long-context sessions ( >1024 tokens) or multi-turn KV reuse expectations

3) Trial logistics & owners:
- Redwood lead: Ben Carter — commercial/coordination
- Tech lead: Aditya Rao — infra/bench config and telemetry
- Success owner (on your side): Olivia (CS/ops)
- Proposed kickoff window: next week (May 17–19) with a 2-week ingest + 1-week acceptance run

4) Next steps (actionable):
- Olivia/Dan: send workload CSV + sample API traces and the evaluation prompt set by EOD Friday
- Redwood (Aditya): provide a synthetic traffic script and baseline runbook by Monday
- We’ll confirm test SLOs and produce a canary schedule once we have the real traces

Attachment: finixa-workload-sample.csv (sample columns: path, avg_tokens, p95_tokens, timestamp, status_code)

Thanks — short replies are fine, just want to lock the inputs so Aditya can size the bench.

—
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0182



From: Olivia Ramos <olivia@finixa.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Dan Li <dan.li@finixa.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 13 May 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Finixa trial readiness — RPS, burst behavior, SLAs & owner assignments

Thanks Ben — good summary. Quick answers below and I attached a short API trace that shows a 10-minute window with a couple of spikes.

Success criteria confirmed with one tweak: p99 <= 600ms for interactive endpoints; for batch scoring we accept p95 <= 1.2s.

Workload inputs (prelim):
- Typical avg RPS: 18 RPS across peak hours
- Peak burst: short spikes up to 160 RPS, typically 30–90s in duration (seen during marketing pushes)
- Concurrency: 8–12 concurrent sessions holding conversation state on a given user cluster
- Token distribution (attached CSV): avg prompt 80 tokens, generation avg 140 tokens, p95 generation 520 tokens
- We do have some long-context cases ~1,800 tokens for retained sessions (low frequency ~0.5% of requests)

Data/privacy note: all payloads will be scrubbed (no PII); we prefer a private pilot if possible but can start on hosted with a strict ACL and audit logging.

Timeline: May 17 kickoff works for us. Acceptance run: 7 days after ingest (two-phase: ingest+stability, and acceptance).

Attachments: finixa-api-trace.json, finixa-eval-set.zip (we'll send via Drive link if you prefer)

—
Olivia Ramos
Head of Integrations | Finixa, Inc.
olivia@finixa.com | +1 (415) 710-2246



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Olivia Ramos <olivia@finixa.com>, Dan Li <dan.li@finixa.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 13 May 2027 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Finixa trial readiness — RPS, burst behavior, SLAs & owner assignments

Perfect — thanks Olivia for the numbers and the trace. A couple of clarifying items and proposed owner actions before the kickoff: 

Clarifications:
- For the 160 RPS bursts: is the 0.5% long-context traffic correlated with bursts (e.g., are those sessions part of spikes) or are they independent? That affects KV cache sizing.

title: NordicHealth - request for transient EU-resident processing & evidence plan
preview:
From: Ida Svensson <ida@nordichealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@nordichealth.com, Johan Ek <j.ek@nordichealth.com>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 08:42:13 +0200
Subject: Request: temporary EU-resident processing & evidentiary items

Hi Ben,

We're prepping for an external privacy audit next month and our security team is asking that all processing for a pilot environment be restricted to EU-resident processing for the duration of the audit window (approx. 6 weeks). We understand Redwood routes some request metadata through non-EU regions for certain analytics/ops tasks; for this pilot our auditors have asked for an exception that ensures logs and debug artifacts remain within EU-only hosts and that any cross-border transfers are documented.

Specifically we need the following before legal signs off on the pilot:
- A list of subprocessors with their processing locations and whether any of them transfer data outside the EEA.
- Confirmation of SCCs in place (or equivalent) and a note on UK IDTA applicability (our legal asked about UK addendum).
- A brief planned timeline + configuration change to ensure EU-only routing for the pilot (start/end dates).

Can Redwood provide these items (or a suggested path) and an estimate for how quickly we can get a temporary routing configuration in place? Happy to jump on a quick call if helpful.

Thanks,
Ida
Ida Svensson
Head of Compliance, NordicHealth

--
Attachment: redwood_initial_qs.txt (text summary of questions)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 09:15:02 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Request: temporary EU-resident processing & evidentiary items

Team — forwarding NordicHealth's ask. Short timeline (audit in ~6 weeks). High level: customer wants EU-only processing for pilot and evidence (subprocessor map, SCCs, note on UK addendum/IDTA).

Key questions for Legal/Privacy:
- Can we provide an attestation on temporary EU-only routing without executing new agreement language?
- Do we need a TIA/DTIA for this scope or will an updated subprocessor map + executed SCCs suffice?
- If UK addendum applies, what is the minimal evidence packet we can deliver to procurement quickly?

I've attached the redwood_initial_qs.txt and a working subprocessor map (v2) from sales ops. 

Ben
Account Exec — Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

--- Forwarded message ends ---


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 10:12:47 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: temporary EU-resident processing & evidentiary items

Ben — thanks for the ping.

Short answer: yes, we can support a time-bound EU-resident processing configuration and provide the evidence package, but there are a few items we should align on to avoid over-promising to the customer. Below is the minimal acceptable package I recommend we deliver within 7 business days, and follow-ups that will require 2–3 weeks.

Minimum deliverable (fast lane, ~7 business days):
- Subprocessor map (current snapshot) with processing country columns — attach XLSX (sales ops provided).
- Confirmation of SCCs: for the product data flows we use the Commission-approved SCCs; provide executed SCCs or an explanation of how they are implemented (metadata, logs).
- Written attestation that Redwood will apply temporary EU routing for customer traffic and debugging artifacts for the pilot window (we can provide a short, signable waiver).
- High-level TIA summary: a short paragraph describing residual transfer risks and mitigations (caching, prefix-only KV retention, no long-term storage).

Follow-on items (2–3 weeks):
- Formal Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA/DTIA) to include supplier questionnaires for subprocessors that may route outside the EEA.
- If UK processing is in scope for any part of the pilot, we will need to confirm UK IDTA/UK Addendum acceptance — customer legal typically signs the addendum or we provide equivalent safeguards.
- Any subprocessor contractual changes (if a subprocessor will move data cross-border) — these will require time.

Operational notes:
- Engineering can implement a per-customer routing policy (region pinning) for the pilot; estimate ~3 business days after legal signs off on the waiver.
- We should limit retention of diagnostic KV caches and logs for that tenant to the agreed pilot window; ops will need the list of endpoints to adjust.

Next steps I propose:
1) Ben to propose the 6-week pilot window dates and confirm which product environments (staging vs prod).
2) I will draft a short signable temporary waiver + evidence packet template (attestation + subprocessor map + SCC note) and circulate for internal sign-off.
3) Marissa to prepare the TIA checklist template and identify which subprocessors require supplier questionnaires.

Happy to review the draft waiver before it goes to customer.

Regards,
Laura Bennett
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Compliance
Redwood Inference

--
Laura's signature


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ida Svensson <ida@nordichealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, procurement@nordichealth.com
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 16:03:21 -0700
Subject: Re: NordicHealth - request for transient EU-resident processing & evidence plan

Ida — thanks for the clear ask and for your patience while we pulled the materials together. Per our legal review, Redwood can provide a time-limited EU-resident processing configuration and an evidence packet to support your audit. Summary below and next steps: 

What we're delivering (attached):
- Subprocessor map (subprocessor_map_v2.xlsx) — includes processing locations and a notes column for cross-border flags.
- Temporary EU Routing Plan (redwood_temp_eu_routing_plan.pdf) — ops steps and estimated cutover/rollback timeline.
- Short attestation/waiver template for signature confirming we will, for the pilot window, keep customer traffic and debug artifacts resident in EU hosts and limit retention to the agreed window.

Notes on SCCs / UK addendum:
- We use the EU Commission SCCs as the baseline for transfers out of the EEA. For any UK-specific processing, the UK IDTA / addendum will be needed; if NordicHealth requires UK addendum acceptance we can include it in the evidence packet but it will require sign-off by customer legal.
- We're preparing a brief TIA summary that highlights mitigations (no analytic exports outside EU, KV cache scoping, encryption at rest/in transit). Full DTIA / supplier questionnaires will follow if your team needs deeper review.

Timeline and action items:
1) Please confirm pilot window (suggested: 2027-06-07 to 2027-07-19).
2) If agreed, confirm the environments to be pinned (staging/prod), and provide any external endpoints we should exclude.
3) If procurement is comfortable, sign the attached short waiver via DocuSign: https://docusign.com/ND-2027-117/temp-eu-waiver

Attachments: subprocessor_map_v2.xlsx, redwood_temp_eu_routing_plan.pdf

If you'd like a 20-minute review call to walk through the packet, I'm available next week.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

> Thanks — that timeline looks promising. We'll review the evidence packet and loop in procurement.
>
> Ida

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Dana Li <dana.li@fintechco.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 15:08:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo recap: integration scope & next steps

Hi Dana — great conversation today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we stay aligned:

- Integration scope: real-time inference (API), prefix caching for historical prompts, and tokenized embeddings for search ranking.
- Success criteria: sub-250ms 95th perc latency on typical queries, < 0.03 USD per 1k tokens estimated post-quantization.
- Risks called out: audit/logging requirements and on-prem enclave for regulated workflows.

Requested next steps:
1) I attached the draft SOW (RW_SOW_v3.pdf) and our current pricing sheet (pricing-sheet-2027Q2.xlsx). Please share with procurement and counsel.
2) Can you confirm who on your side will own the legal redlines and an expected target date for counsel feedback?
3) If helpful, we can schedule a 30-min legal-to-legal call to walk through the key clauses (data retention, audit logs, indemnity).

If procurement has a preferred timeline, call that out and we’ll try to accelerate. I’ll follow up in a few days if I don’t hear back.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0198

Attachments: RW_SOW_v3.pdf (docx/pdf), pricing-sheet-2027Q2.xlsx (spreadsheet)


From: Claire Morris <claire.morris@fintechco.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Dana Li <dana.li@fintechco.com>, procurement@fintechco.com
Date: Wed, 12 May 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Post-demo recap: integration scope & next steps

Ben — thanks for the recap. We circulated the SOW to counsel and attached their initial redlines for your review (FintechCo_Redlines_v1.pdf). Summary of the principal items counsel flagged:

- Data retention: request for fixed retention window and stronger controls for deletion (they want a 90-day minimum retention clause changed).
- Audit logs & access: require audit trail of model inputs/outputs for regulated accounts and a list of fields that must be redacted.
- Indemnity & liability cap: asking to narrow Redwood’s indemnity for third-party IP, and proposing a mutual cap tied to annual spend.

Procurement is aiming to complete legal review by end of next week (week of May 17) but that may slip depending on the number of redlines. If Redwood could propose alternative language for the retention clause and provide a short justification for the indemnity position, that would speed things up.

title: Elara Security Questionnaire — data geography, retention, and erasure flow
preview:
From: Danielle Park <danielle.park@elarahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Elara security questionnaire — data geography & retention (hosted API)

Hi Ben,

Thanks for taking the SQ. Attached is Section 4 of our security questionnaire — we need specific answers for the hosted API portion for our security committee. Key asks are below; we have them in the spreadsheet but a short write-up would be helpful for audit threads: 

- Where (region/physical) is customer request/response data stored by default for hosted API? Is any part of the request persisted outside the region?
- What is the default retention period for PII in request logs, model inputs/outputs, and operational traces?
- If a customer requests full erasure for an account, what is the technical flow and SLA? Include backups/snapshots.
- Are there options to pin data to a specific geography (EU-only, US-only)? If yes, what is the process/lead time?
- Can you provide a simple architecture diagram and any artifacts (SOC2, data flow) that show where data is written and for how long?

We'd like a response for Review by EOD Thursday if possible. Attaching the spreadsheet: Elara_SQ_Section4.xlsx.

Thanks,
Danielle
Security Program Manager
Elara Health

--
Note: this is for hosted API (not Dedicated).


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Danielle Park <danielle.park@elarahealth.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 11:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Elara security questionnaire — data geography & retention (hosted API)

Hi Danielle — thanks for the clear list. Below are concise, customer-ready answers; I also attached a redwood data diagram for context (redwood_data_flow_v1.pdf). Happy to expand any section.

1) Default storage geography
- Default: request/response payloads for the hosted API are stored in the region that corresponds to the serving endpoint (e.g., us-west-2 for US customers by default).
- No automatic cross-region replication of customer request payloads occurs for normal operation. Operational metadata for orchestration (non-PII telemetry) may be replicated to our control plane in a secondary region for resilience, but that metadata does not include model inputs/outputs.

2) Default retention windows
- Short-term request cache: up to 30 days (used for KV/prefix caching and short troubleshooting).
- Audit/operational logs (auth events, API call traces without payloads): 90 days by default.
- Backups/snapshots of model-serving state: retained up to 180 days; snapshots are encrypted and purged on a rolling schedule.
- Model inputs/outputs (hosted): by default, we retain ephemeral copies used for immediate serving and short-term cache only (30 days) unless a customer opts into longer telemetry for quality/evals.

3) Erasure flow & SLA (hosted API)
- Customer-initiated account erasure triggers the following pipeline: (a) mark account for deletion; (b) remove active data from primary region storage within 48 hours; (c) purge short-term caches and KV stores within 30 days; (d) schedule backup/snapshot deletion on next maintenance window — backups removed within 90 days of the erasure request in typical cases.
- SLA: standard Hosted plan — final deletion of all backups and snapshots within 90 days. We can include contractual addenda for expedited erasure (e.g., 30 days) for enterprise customers on Dedicated or Private deployments.

4) Geography pinning
- Yes — customers can request geo-pinning (e.g., EU-only). For hosted customers this is a provisioning/configuration change: lead time typically 2–4 weeks depending on tenancy. For strict legal/regulatory needs we recommend Redwood Private or Dedicated where we can guarantee residency controls and SLA terms.

5) Artifacts
- Attached: redwood_data_flow_v1.pdf (simple diagram) and we can provide SOC 2 Type II attestation on request under NDA.

Next steps: I can convert these bullets into the spreadsheet you sent if that is easiest for your reviewers — want me to populate Section 4?

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0123

Attachments: redwood_data_flow_v1.pdf


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 12:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Elara security questionnaire — data geography & retention (hosted API)

Ben — quick internal clarifications to add before I send final version to Danielle (so we don't overpromise):

- For point (2) above please explicitly call out that 'operational logs' exclude full request/response payloads unless the customer has opted into telemetry.
- For the erasure flow, add that legal holds can extend deletion timelines; we require written notice and we surface any hold to the customer as soon as it's lifted.
- For backups: note that physical media/replication policies at cloud provider level may impose additional retention outside our direct control for a short reconciliation window; however, we remove references at the Redwood layer and request provider snapshot deletion on our cycle.

I'll cc Marissa if she needs to file an addendum for expedited erasure pricing, but let's keep the first send focused on the standard hosted commitments.

-kim

Hi Danielle — quick answers to 1–3 and next steps. See attached consolidated PDF (elara_response_summary.pdf) that you can drop into your workbook; it includes a redacted example deletion log and the chart below.

1) Primary-region removal SLA: We remove customer-identifiable data from primary region storage within 48 hours of an authorized erasure request. We will provide a deletion confirmation record that includes a timestamp and job id once that background job completes.

2) 30-day full erasure contractual clause: For hosted API, the standard operating model is 90 days for final backup purging. We can offer a 30-day contractual commitment for hosted customers with a signed addendum tied to a revenue/commitment threshold (or customers can use Dedicated/Private for guaranteed 30-day windows). I copied approximate addendum options below:
- Option A (Hosted expedited): 30-day full erasure with a 10% uplift on hosted monthly usage, 1-month minimum term.
- Option B (Dedicated): tenancy-level 30-day guaranteed erasure as part of Dedicated contract (no uplift; reserved capacity pricing applies).

3) Audit artifact: attached is a redacted deletion confirmation sample and the processes we follow to reconcile cloud-provider snapshot deletions. It describes the job id, timestamp, and the reconciliation window (up to 7 days for provider acknowledgements).

Next steps: I can (a) populate your spreadsheet Section 4 with the exact phrasing, (b) loop in Legal for an addendum draft if you want Option A, or (c) schedule a 30-min call with our compliance team to walk your DPO through the deletion proof and artifacts. Which do you prefer?

- Addendum: regional SLA uplift to 99.5% + data residency attestation — one-time add-on USD 4,500

Option B — Tiered discount (procurement-friendly):
- 4-week pilot: standard rate (no discount)
- 6-week pilot: 8% discount on usage and embedding units
- 8-week pilot: 12% discount on usage and embedding units
- SLA uplift and data residency attestation require the same one-time add-on (USD 4,500)

Date: Wed, 12 May 2027 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Recap and next steps — Key ownership, KMS options & vendor questionnaire

Hi Maya / Novagen SecOps team,

Thanks for the conversation this morning — appreciate you carving out the time. Quick recap from our side and proposed next steps so we keep momentum: 

What we covered (short):
- Compliance posture: we discussed SOC 2, GDPR scope, and HIPAA applicability for the project.
- Key management options: Redwood-managed CMKs, customer-managed keys (BYOK import into customer cloud KMS), and HSM-backed custom key store patterns.
- Data residency: region selection and per-region KV cache considerations.
- Auditability: what telemetry/audit logs we can provide for key usage and admin ops.

Requests we heard from you:
- Confirmation on SOC 2 status and when Type II will be available.
- Clear statement on whether key material can be exported and how rotations are handled.
- A completed vendor security questionnaire (we should kick that off).

What we can provide now:
- Attached: redwood-kms-options.xlsx — short matrix comparing the three key custody models and high-level tradeoffs.
- Attached: SOC2-status-summary.pdf — current snapshot of our compliance timeline and the controls we expect to include in the Type II report.
- A short answers pack to help with the vendor questionnaire (I can drop a pre-filled brief).

Proposed next steps:
1) We’ll fill the vendor questionnaire you attach (or if you prefer, we can provide a short Q/A brief first).
2) If acceptable, Novagen signs the DPA/NDA (we can send a standard copy) so we can share the SOC 2 Type II report when it’s available.
3) Technical deep-dive session on BYOK/HSM options (30–45m) — propose next Wed 5/19 or Thu 5/20.

Timeline ask from Novagen: please confirm when you need the questionnaire completed (you mentioned two weeks on the call).

Happy to adjust priorities — thanks again.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0182

Attachments: redwood-kms-options.xlsx, SOC2-status-summary.pdf


From: Maya Singh <maya.singh@novagen.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: secops@novagen.com
Date: Wed, 12 May 2027 12:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Recap and next steps — Key ownership, KMS options & vendor questionnaire

Ben — thanks for the clear recap. That matches our notes. A couple clarifications and requests: 

1) SOC 2: we are targeting vendor attestations in Q3. For procurement we need a firm ETA — does Redwood expect Type II before end of Aug 2027? If not, we need to understand compensating controls.

2) Key custody specifics: can you confirm whether keys are ever exportable from Redwood-managed HSMs? Also need clarity on rotation policy (automatic or customer-driven) and whether multi-region replica keys are supported without key material leaving the customer's control.

3) Questionnaire: attaching Novagen_Vendor_Q_v1.docx (please complete and return). We need the completed doc routed to both me and our procurement lead; procurement will want legal language for BYOK and any required attestations.

Timeline: procurement is asking for the completed questionnaire in two weeks (by 2027-05-26). If you need more time, tell us now.

Lastly, can you surface any customer-facing security docs (whitepapers, implementation notes) that specifically describe BYOK flows and HSM integrations? That will help our SecOps review.

Thanks,
Maya Singh
Director, IT Security | Novagen Therapeutics
Maya.Singh@novagen.com

Attachment: Novagen_Vendor_Q_v1.docx


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Maya Singh <maya.singh@novagen.com>, secops@novagen.com
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 12 May 2027 14:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Recap and next steps — Key ownership, KMS options & vendor questionnaire

Maya — thanks, inline answers below and attaching a brief we can drop into your vendor questionnaire to speed things up. I put answers in bold and included the doc vendor-security-brief-answers.pdf.

On SOC 2 timing:
- Current status: SOC 2 Type II engagement is underway; expected attestation delivery: late August 2027. We will provide the report under NDA as soon as it is available. (See SOC2-status-summary.pdf attached.)

Key custody / export policy:
- Redwood-managed CMKs: key material never leaves our HSM boundary. Export of key material is not permitted. Customers can choose Redwood-managed CMKs when they want Redwood to handle key lifecycle but still get KMS audit logs.
- BYOK (customer-managed keys): customer can import key material into their own cloud KMS and then configure Redwood to reference that key via a dedicated key store (supported clouds: AWS KMS custom key stores, GCP CMEK patterns, Azure Key Vault integration). In this model, key material remains under customer control — Redwood stores only a reference/ARN and encrypted key identifiers.
- HSM-backed external key stores: we support patterns where customer-managed HSMs (KMIP-compatible) are used; we do not allow wholesale export of key material from the customer HSM into Redwood.

Rotation and multi-region:
- Rotation: automatic rotation can be performed by Redwood for Redwood-managed CMKs on a configurable cadence; for BYOK the rotation is customer-managed (we provide APIs and hooks).
- Multi-region: we support multi-region deployments; when customers choose BYOK, keys are expected to be present in each region’s KMS or custom key store — we do not replicate key material across regions on a customer’s behalf unless it is part of the customer’s own KMS configuration.

Auditability / logs:
- Key usage logs (key id, operation, timestamp, invoking principal) are available; we can include token-level attribution for requests and provide those logs through our observability console and via secure exports.

Attachments and links I’m including:
- vendor-security-brief-answers.pdf — short Q/A file you can paste into Novagen_Vendor_Q_v1.docx.
- redwood-kms-options.xlsx — comparison matrix from earlier.
- Quick links: https://redwood.ai/security/kms-byok-whitepaper (public), HubSpot deal link: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/DEAL-7892

We can return the completed questionnaire by 2027-05-24 to give you a buffer ahead of 5/26. Would you like us to mark any answers as \"requires follow-up\" where procurement/legal review is needed?

Best,
Ben

Attachments: vendor-security-brief-answers.pdf, redwood-kms-options.xlsx

Date: Thu, 13 May 2027 15:30:00 -0700

Laura / Ben — thanks for the ping. Suggested procurement-friendly language below (feel free to paste into the questionnaire):

1) SOC 2: \"Redwood Inference is undergoing a SOC 2 Type II assessment; the anticipated attestation delivery is late August 2027. A copy of the SOC 2 Type II report will be provided to Novagen under NDA upon completion.\"

2) Key export and custody: \"Key material originating in Redwood-managed HSMs is non-exportable. For BYOK models, customers import and manage key material within their cloud KMS or HSM; Redwood stores cryptographic references (identifiers/ARNs) and does not retain plaintext key material. Network-connected custom key stores are supported subject to configuration and mutual security controls; direct key extraction from customer-managed HSMs by Redwood personnel is not permitted without explicit, auditable customer authorization.\"

3) Rotation / multi-region: \"Automatic rotation for Redwood-managed keys is configurable; for customer-managed keys rotation resides with the customer. Customers who require cross-region key availability must provision keys in each region’s KMS per their governance requirements.\"

Additional operational notes:
- SOC 2 report distribution: requires executed DPA/NDA.
- We should mark any statement that implies indemnities or liability limits as \"legal review required.\"

I’m attaching a short implementation note (kms-byok-implementation-notes.txt) that you can hand to Novagen’s SecOps for the technical deep-dive. I can join the technical session next week if needed.

Aditya Rao
Head of Applied Security | Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.ai

preview:
From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@cerebellumhealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@cerebellumhealth.com>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 09:12:00 -0400
Subject: Pilot DPA draft — can we get a scoped 'no-improve' plus prompt ownership confirmed?

Hi Ava / Marissa,

Thanks again for the demo last week — team was impressed. Procurement asked me to send our standard DPA redline for a two-month pilot. Two items we need to confirm before we circulate to our legal:

1) No-model-improvement / no-training commitment for pilot data (we'd like a firm NO-IMPROVE clause and an ops proof path).
2) Explicit ownership of prompts/responses: Cerebellum needs a clear statement that generated outputs and submitted prompts remain our IP and will not be used to improve models.

I attached a redline (Cerebellum_DPA_draft_v3.pdf). Can you confirm operationally: onshore routing, KMS residency, and whether Redwood can provide an evidence packet (logs + config) at pilot end?

If easier, we can sign a short addendum — happy to fast-track.

Best,
Liam
Director of AI Platform
Cerebellum Health

Attachments: Cerebellum_DPA_draft_v3.pdf


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@cerebellumhealth.com>
Cc: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 12:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot DPA draft — can we get a scoped 'no-improve' plus prompt ownership confirmed?

Hi Liam — thanks for sending this over and for the clear asks. Looping in David (privacy/ops) and Marissa (account lead) so we can respond to both legal and procurement items. Quick summary up front: we can support a scoped no-improve commitment for the pilot with operational evidence; there are a few implementation details to confirm.

Requested next steps from our side:
- David: confirm the evidence artifacts we can provide and timeline.
- Marissa: align on contract path (addendum vs redline) and procurement contact.

A few clarifying questions for Cerebellum to speed review:
- Will the pilot data include PHI or regulated identifiers? (affects onshore requirement)

title: Request: Exec sign-off — 5yr Stability Covenant + Price Protection for Helix Systems
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Daniel Park <daniel.park@helixsys.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-09T09:00:00-07:00
Subject: Request: Exec sign-off — 5yr Stability Covenant + Price Protection for Helix Systems

Hi Daniel,

Thanks again for the productive call earlier. Per our conversation, Helix is evaluating a 5-year committed package (reserved capacity + hosted API for mission-critical workloads). They asked for multi-year pricing protection and clear renewal mechanics — specifically: a price lock for the first 36 months, then a modest uplift cap thereafter, and contractual renewal protection so their unit economics aren’t materially degraded year-over-year.

Summary of the ask: 
- Term: 5 years (annual billing)
- Price lock: 36 months fixed unit pricing
- Uplift cap: max +3% / year after month 36 (cumulative)
- Renewal protection: no unilateral price jumps >5% in any renewal without 60-day notice + right to terminate at renewal if increase exceeds agreed cap
- Commit: $3.6M ARR (annualized) with minimum monthly MRR rhythm (attached model)

Hi Carlos / Anna —

Thanks for agreeing to coordinate a joint demo that shows Redwood + LogiSys running on both AWS Marketplace and GCP Marketplace (brief 30–40 minute technical + business overview, followed by Q&A). We have a small set of customers in EMEA that want to evaluate multi-cloud deployment patterns, and showcasing both marketplace flows will help the procurement questions.

Primary ask: can we lock a 45-minute slot next week that works for LogiSys (Lisbon) + AWS rep + our SEs? Below are preferred windows (Ben in PST, Carlos in WEST/EU):
- Option A: Tue 11 May — 09:00 PDT / 17:00 WEST (preferred by LogiSys)
- Option B: Wed 12 May — 07:30 PDT / 15:30 WEST (earlier for our SEs)

Important: we expect heavy cross-cloud discussion; please confirm if Anna (AWS Marketplace) can join as an observer and whether we should loop in GCP marketplace contacts. If attendance is tight, we want an explicit contingency slot and a fallback runbook (shorter demo + recorded snippet to show Marketplace procurement steps). I attached a draft agenda and an availability ics with buffered slots to avoid timezone overlaps.

- Primary use case: inference at scale (multi-tenant product routing; live latency SLOs).
- Data concerns: HIPAA adjacency for a subset of customers + KMS integration.
- Success metrics: sub-150ms p50 for 1k qps, consistent cost per 1M tokens.

To save back-and-forth, I'm doing a \"shotgun pick\": three short, consecutive windows tomorrow — pick one now and we'll lock it. If we don't hear back in 2 hours we'll release the slots. These are tight to protect engineering time.

- Thu 2027-05-13 11:10–11:40 AM PST

title: Request: Summer compute supplement for Data Curation Consortium
preview:
From: Dr. Hannah Li <hannah.li@midwest.edu>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 15:22:00 -0400
Subject: Request: Summer compute supplement for Data Curation Consortium

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the pilot credits you provided earlier this year — the team at the Midwest University Research Consortium (MURC) has been able to validate the data curation pipeline on ~3.2M tokens. We’ve now landed a small NSF-funded extension to finish annotation for the remaining 1.1M tokens, but our institutional procurement timeline means we can’t get a PO in place before the work window closes (mid-June).

Would Redwood be able to provide a short-term supplement (trial/top-up) — roughly $5k of credits or equivalent token allocation — so we can complete the curated dataset and reproduce results for the final paper? We’re a non-profit academic consortium; the work is non-commercial and will be released under an open dataset license. We attached a one-page scope and an estimated usage spreadsheet.

If you need a confirmation letter from the PI or a short MOU, we can provide that quickly. Appreciate any flexibility — happy to hop on a 20-min call if helpful.

Best,
Hannah
Dr. Hannah Li
Research Director, MURC
Midwest University

Attachments:
- consortium_scope.pdf (one-page scope)
- usage-projections.xlsx (estimated token usage and researcher list)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dr. Hannah Li <hannah.li@midwest.edu>

- Would you be able to accept a short-term promo credit plus a simple MOU (we can use our template) that states the dataset is non-commercial and open?

If those answers look good I’ll check with my manager for discretionary support and loop in Priya on CS for onboarding/usage guardrails. If it helps I can also propose a conditional supplement (e.g., $5k credit valid for 60 days) to cover the June window.

Best,
