From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samantha Ortiz <samantha.ortiz@acme-supplies.com>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 11:37:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Fwd: Coupa invite — vendor accepted but POs not visible / upload bounced

Thanks for the heads up, Ben. I checked the vendor profile in Coupa and found two likely causes: 

- Tax ID mismatch: the legal name on our PO is "Acme Supplies Inc." while they registered as "Acme Supplies (DBA QuickParts)". The tax-id (EIN) in their first upload does not match the EIN on file for the PO routing rule, so Coupa is not linking the PO to their profile.
- W9 format: Coupa enforces a searchable text layer for tax docs in our tenant; scanned images without OCR sometimes fail validation. The vendor’s PDF looks like a high-resolution scan.

Action plan:
1) Samantha — please re-send the signed W9 as a flattened PDF (not a TIFF embedded in PDF) or attach it directly to this thread and we’ll upload it on your behalf. If you can provide the legal entity name and EIN in the email body that speeds validation.
2) Ben / Marissa — Procurement can manually attach PO R-78934 to the vendor as a short-term fix so they can see it in their portal; I’ll do that once we receive the corrected W9.

I’ll mark this high-priority and aim to have the manual PO attachment completed within 2 business hours of receiving the corrected W9.

Laura Bennett
Senior Procurement Manager, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai

title: Bluehaven service impact — proposed relief and next steps
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: URGENT: Bluehaven latency spikes — prep customer comms and relief proposal

Team —

Quick summary: Bluehaven reported sustained tail-latency spikes across their /generate route from 08:05–08:48 PDT (they're on us-west). Modeled traffic shows a kernel-level memory fragmentation issue that led to increased GC overhead on our dedicated cluster serving their tenant. Engineering has a live trace and pre-lim heap dump attached.

Impact: ~5% of requests returned >3s LAT after 08:20 for a 45-minute window. Customer reported several failed workflows. They flagged billing sensitivity and have asked for an executive note + proposal for service relief (credit) while we remediate.

Asks:
- Stephanie: can you run a short bridge with infra/serving (I can host if helpful) to confirm mitigation plan and ETA?
- Alex: please pull the initial KV cache logs and confirm whether the leak is tenant-specific or a runtime regression.
- I'll draft the customer-facing exec note and a suggested credit/waiver; want your signoff before sending.

Attachments: bluehaven_heapdump_20270511.log (text), quick-trace-08-20-2027.png (screenshot)

Ben
Ben Carter
Strategic Account Lead, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0198

--



From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 09:43:00 -0700
Subject: Re: URGENT: Bluehaven latency spikes — prep customer comms and relief proposal

Thanks Ben — on it.

I spun up a private bridge with runtime and infra (cc: Alex). Early read: the allocator shows repeated small allocations that aren't reclaimed quickly under the current fragmentation pattern; appears triggered by a new request header path Bluehaven uses (custom metadata header). Not yet sure if this is a model-serving code path regression or a config edge-case.

Immediate mitigation plan (15–45m):
- Roll an allocation-throttle patch to the affected pool (no restart) to reduce transient amplification.
- Enable per-tenant aggressive GC window for Bluehaven (will increase CPU but drop tail latency).
- Place a short-term routing fallback to the compatible model variant for sensitive routes if we see >2s latency in the next 10m.

I can send a concise, executive-facing update once we have the mitigation applied (I expect done by ~10:00 PDT) — recommend offering a targeted credit for the affected hour and a follow-up postmortem with action items.

I attached the quick bridge notes and a minimal credit-template.

Stephanie
Head, Customer Reliability (acting), Redwood Inference
stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com

Attachments: bridge-notes-bluehaven-20270511.txt, bluehaven_credit_memo_draft_v1.pdf

--



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Lee <sarah.lee@bluehaven.ai>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Bluehaven — interim service note and proposed relief

Sarah —

First, I'm sorry for the disruption earlier this morning. We take incidents like this seriously and I want to make sure you have a clear timeline, immediate remediation, and a proposal for how we'll make this right from a service and financial perspective.

What happened (short): between 08:05–08:48 PDT a combination of an allocator fragmentation pattern and a header-driven codepath increased tail latency for a subset of requests on your /generate route. Our on-call and infra team applied a mitigation that reduced tail latency by ~85% within 25 minutes.

What we did:
- Applied a live allocation-throttle and enabled an aggressive GC window for your tenant.
- Deployed a temporary routing fallback to a compatible model variant for sensitive workflows.
- Opened a full postmortem and allocated a working group (engineers + reliability) to deliver a permanent fix.

Commitments and next steps:
- Short-term: we will issue a one-hour credit for the affected window as an interim, plus 10% service credit on your next monthly invoice to acknowledge disruption.
- Medium-term: engineering will deliver a permanent remediation (code fix + QA) and a detailed postmortem within 7 business days. We will share the postmortem and the test plan for your review.
- CTO sync: I'd like to schedule a 30-minute call between our CTO and Bluehaven's platform lead to walk the technical details. Are you available tomorrow 10:00–11:00 PDT?

Attached: service-impact-summary-Bluehaven-20270511.pdf (PDF) — this contains the timeline, key traces, and the provisional credit calculation.

If you'd prefer a different form of relief (longer credit window, invoice waiver, or an operational credit toward Dedicated capacity), we're open to that and can fast-track an approval.

Again — sincerely sorry. Our team is fully committed to preventing recurrence.

Best,
Ben Carter
Strategic Account Lead, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

Attachment: service-impact-summary-Bluehaven-20270511.pdf



From: Sarah Lee <sarah.lee@bluehaven.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: support@bluehaven.ai
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Bluehaven — interim service note and proposed relief

Thanks Ben — appreciate the quick update and the interim mitigation. A few follow-ups from our side: 

1) Can you confirm the exact request ids and examples that hit the >3s tails so our incident log can reconcile?
2) The proposed one-hour credit plus 10% on next invoice is a good start. Can you send an itemized credit memo so we can route internally?
3) We'd like the CTO sync — tomorrow 10:00 PDT works for Bluehaven (please send calendar invite to sarah.lee@bluehaven.ai and our cloudops alias).

Also, please include any notes about whether this is a one-off tenant interaction with custom headers or a larger regression. We need to understand our contractual exposure.

Sarah
Sarah Lee
Director, Platform Engineering, Bluehaven AI



From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Sarah Lee <sarah.lee@bluehaven.ai>
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 14:32:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Bluehaven — interim service note and proposed relief

Sarah —

Thanks for the fast reply and for committing time for the CTO sync. Per your asks:

1) We exported the request ids that exceeded 3s during the incident window and attached a sanitized list to the postmortem drive link (keeps PII out).
2) Attached is the itemized credit memo (bluehaven_credit_memo_draft_v1.pdf) — line items: 1 hour of full-service credit, automatic 10% invoice credit applied to May invoice, and a $0 one-time operational credit that can be converted to Dedicated capacity trial credit if you prefer. Please review and I'll get formal sign-off from finance.
3) Short-term analysis: initial trace shows the header-path combination is a primary trigger; we are treating this as a tenant-specific behavioral interaction for now, but we haven't ruled out a broader allocator edge-case — the working group will validate scope in the postmortem.

Commitments:
- Postmortem and remediation plan to you within 7 business days (drive link included).
- A follow-up customer Q&A doc for your ops team before the CTO call.
- Finance will fast-track the formal credit note once you confirm the draft.

Calendar invite coming now for tomorrow 10:00 PDT (invite includes the bridge info + attached postmortem folder).

Stephanie

title: Pilot runway relief request — micro-release blocker
preview:
From: Erin Lowe <erin@finlytics.ai>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 05 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot runway relief request — micro-release blocker

Hi Dylan,

Quick note from our eng team: after upgrading to your new JS SDK (v1.4.0) this morning we started seeing intermittent deserialization failures in our connector tests. The issue is blocking the micro-release we planned to land this week (it affects our auth token refresh flow + batched requests).

This has pushed our pilot timeline out by at least two weeks. Because we need to complete the release and run a second smoke test window, could Redwood provision a two-week extension on our pilot and a small credit top-up (we estimate ~60k tokens) so we can finish the fix and complete the production-like runs?

I attached a zipped snippet of the failing logs and a short summary in the email body. We can share a call with your SDK engineer this afternoon if that helps.

Excerpt from logs: \"InvalidKVCacheError: unexpected length at token sequence index 128\" (repeat x3 across instances).

Appreciate any runway you can give on this — it’s critical to our demo next Friday.

Thanks,
Erin
Erin Lowe
Head of Integrations
FinLytics Corp
Attachments: finlytics_integration_logs_20270505.zip


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Erin Lowe <erin@finlytics.ai>
Date: Wed, 05 May 2027 11:30:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Pilot runway relief request — micro-release blocker

Forwarding FinLytics' note — their connector tests started failing after a JS SDK upgrade; they flagged an \"InvalidKVCacheError\" in batched requests. They asked for a two-week pilot extension and ~60k token credit to complete a hotfix and re-run production-like tests.

As the AE on this account, I recommend: approve a two-week extension + grant the requested credit (or a slightly smaller amount tied to the fix) so we keep momentum on the deal. FinLytics is on the cusp of a $75k ARR conversion if the pilot succeeds.

Questions for ops/credit: can we issue a one-off promo credit (deal DL-4921) or do we need RevOps approval?

Next steps I propose:
- Quick triage call between FinLytics and our SDK eng (I can host) in the next 2 hours.
- If error is confirmed to be on our SDK patch side, approve 2-week extension + 60k token promo as a goodwill runway.

Ben/Kim: tagging you for approvals and compliance check (PII or storage concerns).

—
Dylan Brooks
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 06 May 2027 10:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Pilot runway relief request — micro-release blocker

Dylan — thanks for the heads up. If this is a regression introduced by our SDK, I support a runway extension. I can approve up to $5k equivalent in promo credit without escalating, which roughly maps to the 60k token ask depending on model selection. If FinLytics needs more than that we should route to RevOps (copying Marissa).

Please include an estimated usage forecast for the two-week window (safety buffer included) and confirm whether the failing traces include any customer PII. If they do, we need to document retention handling and ensure logs were redacted before sharing.

If the triage shows the issue is on FinLytics' integration side, we should still be flexible with the extension but tie the credit to a short success milestone (re-run smoke test and confirm passing).

Ben

Sales Lead, Strategic Accounts

Date: Fri, 07 May 2027 15:22:00 -0700

Echo Ben — quick compliance/ops notes:
- We need FinLytics to confirm there is no PII in the shared logs. Erin, can you confirm logs were redacted before zipping? If not, please remove or send a redacted copy.
- For a two-week extension + credit, RevOps needs a short MAP (mutual action plan) that lists the milestones we expect (patch deploy, smoke test pass, final QA window). If Dylan can collect that, I can fast-track the promo code request.
- Also, confirm whether FinLytics is on hosted API or Dedicated. If they are evaluating Dedicated and will migrate, we should capture that in the MAP (it affects volume estimates).

If everything checks out, I’ll start the promo approval and instruct RevOps to apply a time-bound credit tied to the deal id.

title: Request: conditional trial top-up and co-funded credits for Scaler Labs pilot
preview:
From: Sasha Green <sasha@scaler.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
Cc: Anil Rao <anil@scaler.io>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot extension + conditional top-up request (Scaler Labs)

Ben —

Thanks again for the demo last week. We ran a short pilot integrating Redwood with our inference pipeline and we’re seeing promising results for our recommendation scoring (latency down ~38% vs baseline). The team asked whether Redwood can provide a short promotional top-up to extend the pilot while we finalize procurement.

Request summary:
- Amount: $25,000 USD in platform credits
- Duration extension: additional 60 days post-activation
- Use case: production-like pilot for Recommendation v2 (expected ~1.2M tokens/week)
- Partner co-funding: Scaler is prepared to co-fund 40% of the credits if Redwood will cover the remainder

Conditions we’d like:
1) Credits must be tagged so usage attributes to the pilot (for attribution/forecasting)
2) A simple MOU confirming credit application and reporting cadence
3) Ability to limit spend and set a hard cap

Attached is a short usage export from our PoC and the SOW that outlines milestones. If you can flag internal next steps and timelines that’d help. We’re hoping to activate in the next 10 business days.

Appreciate it —
Sasha Green
Head of Partnerships, Scaler Labs
Attachments: scaler-pilot-usage.csv, scaler-sow.pdf

To: Partner Credits Committee <credits@redwood.inference>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.inference>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 10 May 2027 09:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Pilot extension + conditional top-up request (Scaler Labs)

Forwarding Sasha’s request — Scaler Labs is an SI partner running a high-potential recommendation workload. Key ask: $25k credits, Scaler to co-fund ~40%. They need activation within ~10 business days.

My read / recommendation:
- Approve a conditional split: Redwood $15k / Scaler $10k (60/40). Keeps Redwood exposure limited while showing partner alignment.
- Conditions: tagged promo code (promo:scaler-pilot-2027), hard spend cap, 60-day activation window, weekly usage reporting (first 3 weeks daily), and MOU signed referencing credit application and attribution.
- Duration: credits expire 90 days after activation if unused; activate with account mapping to scaler@scaler.io and hubspot company record.

Attachments from partner are included. Ask: Legal to prepare a lightweight MOU and Finance to confirm how we will bill/forecast these credits. Laura — can you confirm whether $15k is within delegated approval for Partner Credits? Vivek — tagging you for pricing exception if needed.

Thanks,
Ben
Senior Sales Manager
Redwood Inference

2) Credits expire 90 days after activation
3) Usage tagging required (utm_campaign=scaler_pilot_2027) and a telemetry flag so we can pull aggregate token usage for reporting
4) Documentation: Lightweight MOU drafted by Legal (one page) stating credit amount, co-funding split, reporting cadence, confidentiality, and cancellation terms

title: Marketplace pilot — temporary log carveout and backup hold request
preview:
From: Elena Park <elena@brightmarket.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: marketops@brightmarket.com
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 09:12:00 -0400
Subject: Marketplace pilot — short, urgent ask on logs/backups

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the demo last week — BrightMarket is ready to run the two-week marketplace integration pilot starting next Monday (5/17). Before we flip the switch, our security team asked for two narrow exceptions to the standard retention schedule so we can debug historic transaction reconciliation and run fraud analysis during the pilot window.

Requests (timeboxed to the pilot):
- Keep request/response logs associated with marketplace ordering flows for 90 days (instead of Redwood's default 30).
- Delay purging of snapshot backups for pilot-related resources to 120 days before deletion (instead of the usual 30/45 cadence).

We realize this is an exception — we've attached a small CSV mapping of the pilot datasets so you can scope it (pilot-data-map.csv).

Couple of notes:
- We must ensure this is timeboxed and limited to the pilot-specific dataset IDs listed in the CSV.
- Data will include EU resident records; our legal team will need a short DPA amendment and subprocessors list that covers any cross-border processing.

Can your team confirm feasibility and what minimal evidence you need (SOC2 summary, signed amendment, PO)? We're hoping to have confirmation by EOD Friday (5/14).

Thanks,
Elena
Elena Park
Head of Marketplace Integrations
BrightMarket

--- attachment: pilot-data-map.csv (CSV, 4 rows) ---

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Park <elena@brightmarket.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus_klein@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 12:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Marketplace pilot — short, urgent ask on logs/backups

Elena — thanks for the clear scope and the CSV; appreciate the short turnaround.

Quick response: we can likely support a pilot-scoped exception but need Compliance and Legal to sign off on: (1) the exact dataset IDs, (2) retention window/timebox, and (3) the DPA amendment language for cross-border handling.

@Kimberly / @Neha / @Markus — can you take a look at the attached mapping and propose:
- Minimal DPA amendment text (EU cross-border clause + timebox)
- Backup purge timeline and rollback/playback constraints (we need to be explicit about restore windows and what counts as a backup subject to the hold)
- A list of subprocessors that will see the logs/backups for this pilot and whether any data will transfer outside the EU.

If we can produce a one-page amendment and evidence pack (SOC2 summary + subproc list), I can send that to BrightMarket and ask for PO/signature. Elena — is a one-page amendment acceptable for your legal team for a short pilot?

I put the proposed deadline at EOD Friday in the thread. If better, we can fast-track a verbal approval and follow with an executed amendment within 48 hours.

— Ava
Ava Chen
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

--- inline: forwarded attachment pilot-data-map.csv ---

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus_klein@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 14:22:00 -0400
Subject: Proposed language + operational guardrails for BrightMarket pilot (draft)

Team — reviewed the CSV and the ask. Below is a compact package we can send to BrightMarket today; keeps scope narrow and enforces controls.

1) DPA amendment (summary bullet text for cover email)
- Purpose-limited exception: Redwood will temporarily retain and allow access to logs and backups for the BrightMarket marketplace pilot solely for diagnostic and reconciliation purposes.
- Timebox: retention exception applies only to dataset IDs listed in pilot-data-map.csv, for a period of 90 days from pilot start (pilot start date to be confirmed).
- Cross-border: Any transfers outside the EU will be limited to Redwood subprocessors explicitly named in Exhibit A; additional safeguards (encryption in transit + rest, Access logging) apply.
- Termination: exception automatically expires at the end of the timebox; BrightMarket may request earlier deletion with 7-day notice.

2) Operational definitions / backup purge timeline
- Logs: retained in hot store for 90 days; after 90 days logs transition to immutable archive for an additional 30 days then are purged.
- Snapshots/backups: pilot-tagged snapshots will be held for 120 days; restore requests must be submitted via the Redwood Console ticket flow (select 'Pilot: BrightMarket') and include dataset ID + justification.
- Restore SLA for pilot incidents: 48 hours for non-critical, 8 hours for Sev 1 (we will limit concurrent restore operations to 1 per account during pilot).

3) Subprocessors and evidence
- Subprocessors that may process pilot logs/backups: CloudStorageEU (s3-eu-1), AnalyticsBatchProc (grpc-analytics-eu1), BackupVault (vault-eu).
- Evidence: SOC2 Type II summary attached (SOC2_Type2_summary.pdf). We propose including Exhibit A (subprocessor list) as part of the DPA amendment; draft attached (DPA_exhibit_A_draft.pdf).

4) Controls and rollback
- Access restriction: Only named Redwood engineers on the BrightMarket pilot roster can access pilot-scoped logs/backups; console flags will be enabled to create an audit trail.
- Encryption: At-rest encryption enforced; keys scoped to region.
- Audit: Redwood will provide an export of access logs for the retention window on request.

Next steps (proposed):
- Ava: send this package to BrightMarket with the one-page amendment + SOC2 summary.
- Legal: we will prepare a short DPA amendment for signature (target: 48 hours).
- Ops: prepare a console tag and preserve/process pipeline to ensure only pilot datasets receive the altered retention.

I can sign the one-page amendment on behalf of Compliance once Legal adds the final clause on liability cap (standard).

—Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Head of Compliance, Redwood Inference
kimberly_park@redwood.ai
--- attachments: SOC2_Type2_summary.pdf, DPA_exhibit_A_draft.pdf ---

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Park <elena@brightmarket.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha_kapoor@redwood.ai>, Markus Klein <markus_klein@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 11 May 2027 18:04:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Marketplace pilot — temporary log carveout and backup hold request

Elena — good news: we can support the pilot with the following binding items.

Summary (what Redwood agrees to for the BrightMarket pilot):
- Logs for pilot-tagged datasets (see your CSV) retained for 90 days (hot store), then moved to a 30-day immutable archive before purge.
- Pilot snapshots/backups held for 120 days prior to deletion.
- Exception is strictly timeboxed to the pilot (6-month maximum extension if both parties agree in writing) and limited to dataset IDs in pilot-data-map.csv.
- Subprocessors named in Exhibit A will process pilot data; SOC2 summary attached.
- Access, encryption, and audit controls as outlined by Kimberly will be enforced.

Required from BrightMarket to proceed:
1) Signed one-page DPA amendment (I will attach the DocuSign link once Legal finalizes).
2) Purchase order referencing deal BRIGHT-3492 (we can start the pilot once we have a PO; contractual signature can follow within 48 hours if you need immediate provisioning).
3) Confirmation of pilot start date (we used 2027-05-17 in internal tracking).

If that works, I'll put Ops on standby to tag the datasets and flip the console retention flags once PO is received or we have written approval from your legal/procurement.

Thanks — and please tell your security team we took this down to the minimal scope possible. If you want any tightening (shorter hold, narrower dataset IDs), we can do that too.

Best,
Ava
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava_chen@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

- Restore SLA for pilot incidents: 48 hours for non-critical, 8 hours for Sev 1 (we will limit concurrent restore operations to 1 per account during pilot).

3) Subprocessors and evidence
- Subprocessors that may process pilot logs/backups: CloudStorageEU (s3-eu-1), AnalyticsBatchProc (grpc-analytics-eu1), BackupVault (vault-eu).
- Evidence: SOC2 Type II summary attached (SOC2_Type2_summary.pdf). We propose including Exhibit A (subprocessor list) as part of the DPA amendment; draft attached (DPA_exhibit_A_draft.pdf).

4) Controls and rollback
- Access restriction: Only named Redwood engineers on the BrightMarket pilot roster can access pilot-scoped logs/backups; console flags will be enabled to create an audit trail.
- Encryption: At-rest encryption enforced; keys scoped to region.
- Audit: Redwood will provide an export of access logs for the retention window on request.

Next steps (proposed):
- Ava: send this package to BrightMarket with the one-page amendment + SOC2 summary.
- Legal: we will prepare a short DPA amendment for signature (target: 48 hours).
- Ops: prepare a console tag and preserve/process pipeline to ensure only pilot datasets receive the altered retention.

I can sign the one-page amendment on behalf of Compliance once Legal adds the final clause on liability cap (standard).

—Kimberly
